text: Why should they not take actions against these people who are a danger in the traffic? Now I do not believe that the police are out to get the students because they think it is fun to give fines to young people with no money

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists
text: Students and other cyclists are informed about the rules and fees that come with breaking these regulations

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

To conclude I must once again point out that the [alert, straight-backed constables], as the undersigned so neatly calls them, are not giving fines to people without lights on their bicycles because for the fun of it

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Police vs. Cyclists
text: Next time you have hopefully learned something and you do not make the same mistake again

title: Police vs. Cyclists

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: In a letter to the editor, published in Dagens Nyheter, the 11:th of September 1999, Sverker Arfvidsson makes an argument that the study and practise of genetic engineering should, with few exceptions, be forbidden

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: This he justifies by claiming that by tampering with genetic structures, we are violating the "unwritten laws" of nature itself

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Life on earth has evolved trough a long process of natural selection, and human interference with this development, however advantageous it might seem in a shorter perspective, might, according to Sverker Arfvidsson, prove fatal in the long run, giving rise to new and different forms of life, in a way not desirable to humankind

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Genetic engineering is a fairly new field of research, where development has, in many ways, just begun

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: It inspires hope of useful applications and advances in many areas, as well as fear of potentially harmful consequences

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Among some, the latter sentiment is the predominate one; in an article published in Dagens Nyheter, Sverker Arfvidsson, offering his views on the subject, goes so far as to call for a prohibition of virtually all further study of the subject

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Would, then, such a seemingly drastic step be a wise one? Is the apprehension that desires it indeed justified? I would say it is not, and this I shall presently elaborate on

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Scientific study involving genetic engineering is today conducted in many fields, and a number of applications of this research have presented themselves

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Arfvidsson indeed mentions the possibility of manipulating the genetic material of animals and vegetables, in order to make them larger, more resistant to the wrath of the elements, or more fit for human consumption

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Significant as this may be, the most important advances would, however, seem to be those made in the field of medicine

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Using techniques involving genetic engineering, various hormones, insulin for example, and other substances important in the treatment of serious diseases, can be procured cheaper and more efficient than allowed by more traditional procedures

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Diagnosing cancer in its many forms, can be done more easily, and treatment be made more accurate

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Research is underway, aiming at methods for using genetic therapy as treatment for illnesses otherwise difficult to cure

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: These are all examples of ways in which genetic engineering could be used to save human lives, or at least make them more tolerable, but which we according to Sverker Arfvidsson ought nevertheless to abstain from

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Why then, one might ask, should we deny ourselves of possibilities such as these? For Arfvidsson and like-minded sceptics, the answer is indeed simple

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: By tampering with genetic structures we are, they seem to say, violating the unwritten laws of nature, perhaps even treading on ground reserved for divine forces and other supernatural beings

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: But what is it then that makes the metaphysical implications of genetic engineering so very different from those of other scientific and medical procedures, such as, for example, organ transplants? Have not man already, to some extent, assumed control of life and death, and, by the practise of supplying people with second hand, or even mechanical, spare parts, trespassed on this natures domain? Scepticism is, surely, likely to surround most new and spectacular scientific developments, and perhaps also organ transplants, before they became a medical routine measure, attracted critics with arguments akin to those of Sverker Arfvidsson

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Rather than seeing genetic engineering as something unique in this respect, one might question more generally the relevance of this particular kind of criticism, looking at the degree of actual knowledge of the subject in question that it suggests

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Undoubtedly, scepticism towards genetic engineering could be found among persons with a firm knowledge of the area, whose opinions may well be worth considering seriously

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: However, the rather emotional rhetoric of Sverker Arfvidsson, relying more on the dim notion of genetic engineering going against the wisdom inherent in nature, than on factual arguments, does not seem to indicate any such knowledge

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Presumably the scientists researching this field have an insight into this rather complicated matter that renders their views, hopeful or sceptical, more relevant than those of someone lacking a clear understanding of the issue

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: But even if we choose to see the genetic engineers as nothing more than a gathering of mad scientists, blind to the consequences of their actions, could we not rely on nature, in its alleged wisdom, to put things right again, should we poor feeble-minded human beings disturb the natural order of things? Surprisingly, this does not seem to be the case; according to Arfvidsson, genetic engineering could, in the long run, give life on earth a new and different direction, not desirable to humankind

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: This may of course be true; however, one might on the other hand consider that change is indeed nothing new to our world, and does not necessarily have to be for the worse, even when caused by man

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: I, for one, would actually be quite delighted to see a few imaginative additions to our all too well-known flora and fauna; a variation on the familiar theme of squirrels and bears greeting the tired wanderer seeking recreation in the wilderness, the glorious winged wapiti instead of the dreary old deer, dear as it may be

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: In the end, it would seem, this is mainly a question of our ability and freedom to seek knowledge and understanding of ourselves and the world around us

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: In doing so, we must proceed with caution, but to be paralysed by fear would indeed be foolish

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Genetic engineering opens up many fascinating possibilities, but, as it is largely a new and unknown terrain, also demands consideration and scepticism

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: This scepticism, however, should be based on insight, not on ignorance

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: To completely ban all research in this field would only be to give in to narrow-minded distrust of science, and nothing is to be gained from thus putting ourselves in an intellectual strait-jacket

title: Genetic Engineering - a Menace or a Blessing?
text: Therefore, rather than avoiding the fruit of knowledge because dull and menacing voices urge us to do so, we ought to sink our teeth into it!

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: The teacher Olle Ekhöjd claims in his letter to the editor in Dagens Nyheter (Oct 8, 1999:c 4) that there would be problems if a homosexual couple adopted a foreign child, and he is concerned with the difficulties that such a combination could bring

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: He also attacks 14 members of the Parliament who have written an article and criticised organisations of adoption and adopted children of being homophobes when they questioned a proposition written by the Parliament about the right for homosexuals to adopt foreign children

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: Ekhöjd says that the politicians involved do not know what it means for a foreign child to come to a new country, and to allow homosexuals to adopt foreign children would make the problems even bigger for the children

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: I cannot think of one reason why we should not allow homosexual couples to adopt foreign children or why there would emerge any greater problem due to that combination

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: I think that Ekhöjd is completely wrong and I think that homosexuals should be allowed to adopt, both Swedish and foreign children

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: The first thing which I find strange and inaccurate is Ekhöjd's suggestion that adopted foreign children have enough traumas and problems when they come to a new country and a new family, and indirectly he means that it would be too tough for these children if they in addition to these came to a family of two mothers or fathers

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: Swedish homosexuals are not allowed to adopt children at all, but what Ekhöjd claims is that it would be even more unfair for foreign children to come to a homosexual family, I quote; "it is important that they [the foreign children] do not have to meet unnecessarily large obstacles in the process of assimilation

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: " I think this is absurd, the majority of us do not really make a difference between a foreign and a native child anymore because the barriers between nations and races have been broken down

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: Today we do not see foreign children as something unusual or odd, they are being accepted and loved by their Swedish friends and they assimilate easily in society, and we are accepting homosexuals more and more aswell

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: To suggest that homosexual adoptive parents would bring these children to misery is profane and shows lack of knowledge and understanding

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: Homosexuals are just as much human as anyone else, and if given the possibility to raise a child, foreign or not, they would prove just as good parents as the rest of us, some perhaps even better

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: However, I am aware of racism and homophobia, but to forbid homosexual couples to adopt foreign children for this reason is to make the problem seem larger than it really is

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: Further on Ekhöjd asserts that "as long as there are prevalent prejudices against homosexuals we might ask ourselves if it is responsible to let foreign adopted children - who already today have to struggle to not be regarded as unusual - to grow up with two mothers or fathers

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: " What he does here is to turn all the light on the possible problem which in turn makes it a problem

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: To make the prejudices an excuse for forbidding homosexuals to adopt foreign children is to live up to the prejudices and to make them even greater! Ekhöjd also holds that if homosexuals would raise foreign children it would mean "double alienation" for the children, which might be one too much

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: I think that it is only we, the society, who can change these prejudices and make it easier for foreigners and homosexuals to be accepted

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: The last suggestion that Ekhöjd makes is ludicrous; he maintains that "to protect the children against a narrow-minded surrounding, the homosexuals' rights in this case should for now stay at the right of adopting Swedish-born children

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: " What he seems to suggest is that foreign children with another look would be harassed, and even more so if they have homosexual parents

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: But that could be the same problem for a Swedish child with an unusual look and with gay parents

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: We all know that you do not have to be or look strange to become bullied, but to deem the combination of foreign adopted children and homosexuals parents is to realise the supposed complication

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: The development of acceptance of others has been slow but I think that we have come so far in 1999 that it would not be more difficult for foreign children to be accepted into the society because they live with homosexual parents

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: The author of this letter to the editor is afraid that homosexual adoptive parents who adopted foreign children would ruin the wellbeing of these

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: To me it seems like Ekhöjd thinks that society's prejudices are there to be followed, and he is also very prejudiced himself

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
text: I react strongly against his old-fashioned and antipathetic opinion and would like to see the proposition that the Parliament had come true

title: Swedish Homosexual Couples Should be Allowed to Adopt Foreign Children
Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: In Sweden, and abroad Uppsala has been famous for many years for its great university of ancient lineage

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Today there is also something else that is very characteristic for Uppsala and surely everybody who has visited Uppsala have noticed all the cyclers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Uppsala is the city of the cyclers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: The aim of this essay is to argue against the standpoints, which were found in the article "Inte bara cyklar

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
Do Not Blame Cyclers!
Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: " (Upsala Nya Tidning Sep

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: 7, 1999:8) Siw Eriksson, who in this article states that at the present time the situation on the sidewalks of Uppsala is dreadful and cyclers are the reason for this

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: She is very upset with the fact that cyclers chain their bicycles to poles everywhere in the city

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Siw Eriksson is disabled and because of the chained bicycles it is hard for taxi service to reach their customers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: But is it really right to blame cyclers for this? We all know that a great amount of bicycles are stolen in Uppsala every year

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Cyclers have to do what they can to prevent this and the only way of keeping a bicycle in Uppsala is by using strong chains

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It is not the cyclers' fault that there is so few bicycle stands in Uppsala

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It is the local authorities and not the cyclers who are responsible for this

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Today there are people who spend about 8,000 SKr on a bicycle since riding a bicycle is a fast way of tansportation in a city like Uppsala

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: However, 8,000 SKr is a lot of money and just like people lock their cars people want to be positive that their bicycles are still where they left them when they return from work or where ever they have been

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: According to Birgitta Jennische there are many people who fear to be on the on the sidewalks of Uppsala because of all the cyclers who ride their bicycles there

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: She also mentions that these days the bicycles have many gears, but what does that have to do with it? Finally Jennische points out that it is against the law to ride bicycles on the side walks

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Yes and the cyclers are aware of that but just as pedestrians fear cyclers, cyclers also have their worries

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: People on the sidewalks fear cyclers and cyclers fear motorists

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It is not easy to act properly as cyclers in Uppsala because wherever they take their bicycles they seem to be in somebody's way

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: In some parts of the city the cycle tracks are well developed but there is still much to do

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Once again cyclers cannot be blaimed for the lack of cycle tracks

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: If there were more cycle tracks in Uppsala there would be less cyclers on the sidewalks

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: The situation on the streets of Uppsla is indeed very dangerous for cyclers considering the heavy traffic in the city, especially since there are so many busses

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It is not at all surprising that so many cyclers feel that they have to escape to the sidewalks

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: After all the difference between a cycler and a motorist is greater than that of a cycler and a pedestrian

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: There are plenty of sidewalks in every city but very few cycle tracks

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Cyclers are always neglected, and politicians should hear these accusations not cyclers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: The most upsetting argument in this aricle comes from both Siw Eriksson and Birgitta Jennische who express that cyclers behave thoughtless, they do not pay any regards to other people in traffic and they make the sid walks a dangerous place to be at

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Eriksson and Jennische have even discussed the possibility of using the local TV-channel to make cyclers aware of their lack of common sense in traffic

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Why do cyclers have to take all the blame for this situation? Everybody has to be careful in traffic and sometimes people on the sidewalks are a threat to themselves because they do not watch their step, and it is the same thing with motorists who are not always paying as much attention to the traffic as they should

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: As a cycler it is not always easy to know where it is allowed to ride a bike since there is a lack of signs with rules for cyclers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It should be in the best interest of the local government to build sufficient cycle tracks and equip them with signs to help cyclers

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Looking at this from an environmental point of view the city should take care of its cyclers and develop the city of the cyclers so that it can serve as a role model for other cities

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Would people on the sidewalks prefer more cars and busses instead of bicycles? Use your energy to make the situation better for everybody instead of making it worse for cyclers and share your opinions with people who can really do something about it

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: Chaining bicycles to poles, riding their bikes on the sidewalks and acting rude, that seems to be the picture of cyclers in Uppsala

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It almost sounds like this group of people is some kind of terrorist gang

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: However, it is important to remember that cyclers in Uppsala are not only students but also people in the middle of their career, children, and retired people

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: It is this group in general that is accused of not paying any attention at all to other people in traffic

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
text: However, this group can build neither cycle stands nor cycle tracks and therefore they should not have to take this criticism

title: Do Not Blame Cyclers!
Homosexuals adopting?
text: Under the heading of "It's about the child's best" (SvD 5 Oct 1999) seventeen members of parliament argue for a change of the adoption law so that it only looks to what is best for the child

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: As it is today, those homosexual couples that want to adopt a child have to withhold their homosexuality from the authorities and this, they assert, is unacceptable

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: The MP's claim that, according to research, there is no difference between children who has been brought up by homosexual parents and those brought up by heterosexual parents

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: They find it unfair and discriminating that a whole group of potential adoptive parents should be left out from the selection

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Today homosexuality is present in everyday life and it is not as controversial as it was a few decades ago

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Since the partnership law of 1995 the question have been raised about whether homosexuals should be allowed to adopt a child or not

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: This is not a question of being for or against homosexuality; the question is if these couples really can give a child what it needs during its childhood in the form of being a role model and giving security

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: There is, with justice, much doubt about this and many people oppose to a law allowing homosexual couples adoption

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: In this essay I present some of the supporters' arguments to prove the opposite - that the best for the child is if only heterosexual couples are allowed to make adoptions

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: The reason, the MP's say, to why homosexuals are being 'shut out' from the laws of adoption is due to prejudices and taboos that are, since long, rooted in society

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: But these so called prejudices are based on a the thought that homosexual couple cannot have children of their own and placing a child in such a family speaks against a kind basic natural law

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Homosexual persons can of course also have a longing to 'marry and settle down', but however this is looked upon, it is unnatural for a child to have two parents of the same sex

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Moreover, when a heterosexual couple divorce and they are going to decide about the custody of the child, the opportunity for the child to see both parents is important just because a child needs both its parents during childhood: a child in a homosexual couple cannot be different - it needs a mother and a father to identify with

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Another argument is that it is absurd to exclude a whole group on grounds of homosexuality

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: This is not absurd - it shows a concern about the children

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: A child growing up with two parents of the same sex is bound to be confused when he/she realizes that the friends' families does not look quite the same

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: The child also runs a risk of being harassed by fellow pupils and 'friends'

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: It is natural for a child to defend and stand up for its parents in this kind of situation, but this can be too much for a young individual to handle

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: In the end he/she can turn against the parents and accuse them for having put him/her in this situation

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: If the child has been adopted as a baby he/she might find it easier to accept the situation, but if the child has already lived in a family with heterosexual parents the situation gets more difficult

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: (The only way for Swedish homosexual couples to adopt a child is to adopt a foreign child and this child can be a couple of years old

title: Homosexuals adopting?
Homosexuals adopting?
text: Being aware of the situation when adopted, it is easy for the child to again put the blame for its confusion on the parents, since it was their desire to have a child that got him/her in to this rather unusual situation of having two parents of the same sex

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Homosexual parents are as capable as heterosexual ones of giving a child security during its childhood, the MP's argue

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: However, there is something inherently insecure about growing up with homosexual parents

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: The parents are a child's first and strongest role models and the child looks up to them and think, 'when I grow up, I am going to be like them'

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: But somewhere along the road towards 'independence' the child comes to realize that his/her parents way of living does not suit him/her when thinking about the future

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: There is little chance that the child adopted by a homosexual couple should be homosexual as well

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: This is the same process that a child, with heterosexual parents, realising its homosexuality goes through, but the adopted child goes in the other direction through the process

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: What the child has thought of as being a norm suddenly collapses

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: This process, concerning the adopted child, is excepted to take place; the only question is when

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: If this should happen to a teenager, who already has enough problem with finding his/her identity and place in life, the consequences can be serious

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: It is not worth risking that a child get psychological problems just because a homosexual couple wants to become parents, for even though the adoption board finds the future parents suitable, the child might not agree

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: Everybody can say that they are acting for the best of the children - no matter if they are for or against homosexuals adopting - but if one really should consider the children's' needs, heterosexual parents ought to be the only alternative

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: In this way all the children - adopted or not - are given the same starting point, for the most normal situation for a child is to live with two parents of different sexes so that they have the possibility to identify with both sexes

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: They also feel that they are part of a whole since most children have heterosexual parents

title: Homosexuals adopting?
text: The feeling that it is 'me and the other' is not present, as it can be if you have parents different from other children's

title: Homosexuals adopting?
WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: As people these days choose to spend quite a lot of their spare-time in front of the TV screen, it is certainly important that the TV networks broadcast qualitative and stimulating programmes, especially when children are concerned as they are so impressionable

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Unfortunately, TV is, as is everything else, dependent on profitability, and making good TV costs a lot of money

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Hence, the numerous adverts which cause annoying breaks in the middle of our viewing

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: In Sweden we are fortunate to have another system with two advert-free channels and one additional channel with quite little advertising compared to for instance American TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Still I believe we have qualitative TV in Sweden

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: The managing director for TV4 Uppland, Jan Nordling, expresses quite a different opinion in an article in UNT 12th October

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Nordling argues that the Swedish TV networks need to change their way of making TV in order to become more profitable and that way be able to make better TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: The reason why Swedish TV needs to improve, he says, is that otherwise it cannot compete with the satellite channels and American TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: He does not even mention for instance British TV which actually makes quality-TV worth competing with, but I agree with his statement that more Swedish programmes should be produced and shown on TV, but the other measures he suggests are absurd

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: He wants to "specialise" Swedish TV in the sense that SVT should be given a very restricted commission, while TV4 should become broader, having more to offer for a big audience and being in charge of the profitable entertainment

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: SVT would then become the "culture channel" with only cultural programmes, documentaries, TV-theatre, debate programmes and the news

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: A distinct choice for a distinct audience

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: In order to be able to handle the more costly entertainment, TV4 should then have the same advertising regulations as the satellite channels i

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: e

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: more breaks, but they should also be allowed to introduce opinion adverts, political adverts, religious adverts and adverts intended for children

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Nordling asserts that any ethical questions on the matter can be dealt with by the network

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Firstly, we viewers are sick of adverts already as it is now, we certainly do not need any more interruptions in our viewing

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Sometimes it feels like the adverts take up almost as much time as the programmes

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: If Nordling's wishes would come true we would have to watch even more adverts, given that we want to watch something other than documentaries and the news

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Many of the adverts themselves would also become even more annoying with religious sects and various politicians spraining their tongues every 15th minute

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Secondly, I have a feeling that the network would not care so much for ethics as long as the money come rolling in

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: What next? Nazi adverts? No, there has to be a limitation of what is allowed to be advertised or else we will drown in adverts expressing opinions, one worse than the other

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Our patience with adverts has stretched far enough already, we do not want any extra ingredients aggravating the situation

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: And what about Nordling's idea about children's' adverts? Nordling says that children are not as impressionable as we think, but I think that he is wrong

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Children are very impressionable, especially if it is their favourite cartoons conveying the message, and I do not think that children should be exposed to temptations every time they turn on the telly

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: They do not have the same ability as adults when it comes to separating TV from real life

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Furthermore, why should we change our way of making TV in order to compete with American TV? Nordling states that Swedish TV needs to improve, hinting at American TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Is American TV really that good? Certainly, a lot of American programmes are good, but if you consider for instance the American programmes shown on the satellite channel TV3, most of it is meaningless pastime or plain rubbish

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Is it that kind of TV Nordling strives for? Because I think that that is exactly what he is going to get if Swedish TV goes commercial, and therefore I do not see any reason why the Swedish networks should "go American"

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Maybe it is even fortunate that Swedish TV is not so profitable, that way we only have the money to make the good TV and none to spare for the rubbish

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Actually, when I think about it, maybe Swedish TV is more profitable than American TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: It all depends on how you look at it, profitable as in producing money or profitable as in producing a useful result

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Swedish TV is good, let us keep it that way

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: A SUMMARY OF THE ARTICLE "ORIMLIGA REGLER HINDRAR TV4" In an article in UNT 12th October, the managing director of TV4 Uppland, Jan Nordling, argues that when the agreement between TV4 and the Swedish State expires in 2001 it is vital to reword the advertising regulations

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: It is necessary, he states, in order to further the growing "digital environment" and to make Swedish TV more qualitative, more profitable and better able to compete with American TV

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: In his opinion, SVT should be given a more restricted commission while TV4 should become broader, and he therefore demands that TV4 should have the same advertising regulations as the satellite channels

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
text: Moreover he suggests that the sanction fees paid by TV4 to the State should be done away with, and instead local TV should be promoted by financial support from the Swedish Radio Fund

title: WHAT IS PROFITABLE TV ANYWAY?
Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Recently an editorial that appeared in Svenska Dagbladet (1999-10-03) stated that homosexual couples must not be given the right to adopt children

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: According to the writer, adopted children are very vulnerable

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: They have been separated from their biological parents; they have often had several caretakers; and they have left their native country at an early age

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Growing up in a family that differs from other families would be adding to their trauma

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: In this essay I intend to argue against this view

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: I do not think that having two parents of the same sex necessarily has to be a bad experience, which the author of the editorial seems to imply

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: People who are homosexual have experienced being cast as different, and that experience can benefit a child who feels rootless

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: The author of the editorial does not seem to think that homosexuals are worse parents than heterosexuals (or at least s/he does not say so); he is afraid that the child will be harassed for having parents that are homosexual

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: But according to Niclas Berggren, a member of RFSL's (the national organization for sexual equality) board of directors, research has shown that children growing up with gay or lesbian parents are not worse off than are children with heterosexual parents, and they are able to make friends as easily (Svenska Dagbladet 1999-10-07)

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: It is cynical to deny gays and lesbians the right to adopt because their child may be teased; all children suffer the risk of being teased

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: We see then that having homosexual parents need not be traumatic

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: There is every reason to believe that homosexual couples can make just as good adoptive parents as heterosexual couples can

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: The Swedish authorities interview couples who wish to adopt and their suitability as parents is carefully considered

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Assuming that homosexual couples will be given the right to adopt eventually, those that pass these tests may actually have advantages over many heterosexual couples

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Berggren argues that their experiences of being homosexual in a society where heterosexuality is the norm will help them understand the feelings of alienation that their child may feel growing up

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: The "coming out" experience have afforded them maturity and knowledge of what it is like not to be completely accepted by society, and an adopted child may benefit greatly from this

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Many Swedes who were adopted from another country feel rootless as children, and feel that they do not quite belong in the Swedish society

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Because of this, Svenska Dagbladet's editorial argues, they must grow up in a safe and peaceful environment

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: I think that gay and lesbian couples are just as capable as heterosexual parents of giving them that

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: The editorial writer goes on to say that the gay and lesbian movement is trying to use children as tools for gaining legal rights and acceptance from mainstream society

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: This implies that people who are homosexual have ulterior motives for wanting the right to adopt, which I think is very unfair to assume

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: I cannot believe that a homosexual couple that wishes to adopt a child does so because they want to further a cause

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: They do it because they long for a child and know that they are able to provide a good and loving home environment for him or her

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: That must be the starting point, and it is in itself reason enough to give homosexual couples the legal right to adopt

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: If we accept that they can be just as good parents as anyone else, we must give them that right; and, in time, because they will be fairly common, families with two parents of the same sex will not be looked upon as abnormal

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Naturally this process will take time, but I feel certain that it will happen

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Today many households consist of a single parent and a child, or unmarried couples with children, all constellations that were judged inappropriate not long ago

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Granting homosexual couples the right to adopt would be admitting that they are just as fit to be parents as are heterosexuals, and it would be an important signal to the rest of the world

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: The author of the editorial argues that it does not matter what Sweden decides; no country in the world will give up its children for adoption to a country that allows gays to adopt

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: This is unfortunate, but I still think that Swedish lawmakers should set a good example

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: If we accept that gays and lesbians are just as good parents as are heterosexuals, should we not focus on changing people's attitudes toward homosexuality instead of fiercely denying people who are homosexual rights that everyone else has? I agree that children should not be used to sway opinions

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Gays should be given the right to adopt because they are able to provide a good environment for the child, not in order for them to gain acceptance by mainstream society

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: However, granting them that right would also have the good effect of normalizing families with parents of the same sex, and it would be a powerful statement to all homophobes

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Summary In an editorial entitled "Adoptivbarn är inte verktyg" (Svenska Dagbladet 1999-10-03), the author argues that the gay and lesbian movement is trying to use children as tools for gaining legal rights and acceptance from mainstream society

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: S/he feels that adoption should be about what is best for the child, and not what is best for people who are homosexual

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: According to the author, adopted children are very vulnerable

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: They have been separated from their parents and natural surroundings at an early age, and growing up in a family that differs significantly from other families would be adding to their trauma

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
text: Furthermore, all adoptions have to be approved by the child's native country, and there is no country that would allow its children to be adopted by gay or lesbian couples in Sweden

title: Granting Homosexual Couples the Right to Adopt
Restrained by absurd rules?
text: "TV4 is restrained by absurd rules" was the headline of an article printed in the debate section of UNT on 12 October

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Its author, Jan Nordling, managing director of TV4 Uppland, argues that it is about time that television politics of Sweden were radically changed

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: When, at the turn of the year 01/02, a new agreement will be settled between TV4 and the state he wants to see less retarding requirements on commercial television

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: In his opinion, the main goal with the new settlement must be to assure a definite rise in profitability for TV4

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Personally, however, I want to assert that it is highly impreferable, in a wider perspective, to promote a development where market forces get to dominate the basis of all decisions

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Jan Nordling wants the present rules that regulate commercials within TV4 to be adjusted so that they are much more similar to the ones that regulate Swedish satellite channels

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He claims that it is utopian to avoid commercial breaks in a commercial television channel and suggests shorter, but in turn more frequent, interruptions

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: According to Jan Nordling, this would be more attractive to the people who are watching, but I am not convinced

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: The main reason why he wants to see this change is undoubtedly the fact that it would be more attractive to advertisers and hence boost TV4's economy

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: The managing director of TV4 Uppland is also of the opinion that TV4 should be allowed to follow international custom in its advertising

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He claims, for example, that the Swedish prohibition of commercials aimed at children is completely unfounded

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: "Children are not that gullible and have not got the economic force necessary for such prohibitions to be motivated"

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: If the goal is not to manipulate these children, then what is it? Children are just as easily influenced as everyone else is

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: The difference, that motivates the present rule in Sweden, is that they cannot be expected to have the same knowledge about potential effects of commercials as grown-ups have

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Small children can sometimes not even distinguish commercials from normal programs and hence cannot, in the same way as adults very often do, actively choose not to be manipulated by simply stop watching

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: When it comes to the economic force then it is quite evident that it is their parents' force that has to be considered here, not their own one

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Jan Nordling thinks, furthermore, that the Swedish ban on advertisements for pure opinions or viewpoints is a much awkward exception to the more general rule we have in Sweden about freedom of speech

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He takes the discussion one step further by also questioning why political and religious commercials should be banned

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: These reflections are interesting and quite understandable in a way, but since the effects of commercials have been proved, more than once, these prohibitions are justified

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: If they were not there we would very likely face a situation where we were almost daily exposed to pure propaganda

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Jan Nordling recognizes, certainly, that ethical considerations in many cases are motivated, but is of the opinion that it from case to case should be up to every company itself to make the judgements and decisions necessary

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Personally, I think that it is better to be safe than sorry in this sort of situation

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: I would like to assert that no matter how strict the present rules may be it is better to keep them than to run the risk of facing a disastrous development

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: The only reason why Jan Nordling wants it to be up to every company itself to make the ethical judgements is that he realizes that it would be a way for TV4 to bring in more money

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Why, I am quite convinced that they would choose to be much less strict than today

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: According to Jan Nordling, the main reason why it is important to assure a definite rise in TV4's profitability is that domestic production of television programs must be ensured

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: "To assert Swedish culture in the world of television is important, but perhaps even more important is that television in more ways than today depicts a Swedish multi-cultural reality"

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: This sounds all very nice, but exactly what Swedish culture is it that TV4 will depict through its broadcasting of an even greater number of soap operas and other forms of light entertainment? Are these types of program really essential for us in the way that he wants us to believe? Furthermore, in what way would they depict a multi-cultural reality? Would minorities in Sweden be better represented if TV4's main goal is to ensure as high a profitability as possible? The managing director of TV4 Uppland is also of the opinion that the company's concession fee should be done away with

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He finds it absurd that TV4 in this way pays for some of the programs broadcasted by SVT, which very clearly reveals his double standard of morality

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He has just paid a warm tribute to the types of program that SVT actually produce by, among other things, saying that it is essential to depict a multi-cultural reality

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: How can it then be absurd to support the activities of SVT? To me, such an utterance makes his previous statements ring even more hollow

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: To summarize, the main goal with the coming settlement between TV4 and the state must, in Jan Nordling's opinion, be to ensure a definite rise in profitability

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: By simply analyzing his main ideas, presented above, we can see clearly for ourselves that it all boils down to money

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: I think that we should be proud, rather than anything else, of the present rules that regulate the activities within TV4

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: They are there to protect individuals and to consider them being absurd is, to me, quite absurd in itself

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: Summary Jan Nordling, managing director of TV4 Uppland, asserts in the article "TV4 is restrained by absurd rules" (UNT Oct

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: 12, 1999) that it is about time that television politics of Sweden were radically changed

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: In his opinion, the main goal with the new agreement, that in the near future is to be settled between TV4 and the state, must be to assure a definite rise in TV4's profitability

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: In order to boost their economy he believes that it is essential to change the rules that regulate advertising within the channel

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
text: He also finds it necessary to abolish the concession fee that TV4 presently is obliged to pay to the state

title: Restrained by absurd rules?
Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: In the article I have chosen, "Ökad biltrafik bra för miljön" (Dagens Nyheter Oct

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: 2, 1999:A4), Tom Corneliusson and Björn Gillberg argue that an increasing amount of car traffic on the bridge of Öresund actually will be of benefit for the environment

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Further the bridge is considered to benefit for example cultural and educational interchange between regions

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: As high a fare as has been proposed for the crossing would however disturb the positive effects and curb the trips via bridge, a trend which they, through this article, want to obstruct

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: On several points I do not find their arguments convincing though

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: In the first paragraphs of the article, Corneliusson and Gillberg assert that there are no reasons for limiting traffic on the bridge as regards environmental matters

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Furthermore they suggest that car traffic, although increased to the highest possible level, still will cause less pollution than the ferry service, why an increased rate of crossings rather should be encouraged by lower fares than the opposite

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: How this can be explained, is with the fact that almost all cars by the year 2010 will have catalytic converters

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Something they do not take into account however, is that ferries also will have improved technique to limit pollution to a minimum

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Car-production technique of tomorrow is compared to ferry-one of today, which is not a fair means of comparison

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Corneliusson and Gillberg's next statement is that exhaustion in the whole region of Öresund will not increase, to strain the question it will in fact decrease compared to today

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: A statement like this is based on calculations on a "worst conceivable case", when the increase of traffic in Skåne would be 70% over a period of 20 years (from 1990-2010), but the decrease of harmful emissions as large as 80%

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Why this can be the case, concerning elements such as nitrogen oxide, sulphur dioxide, carbon oxide, and carbon hydrogen, can also be explained with catalytic converters

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: A fact not mentioned by the two authors of the article, is that we already today let out too much carbon dioxide - an element that contributes to the greenhouse effect

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Thus an increased amount of traffic of course means more discharge of carbon dioxide

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Corneliusson and Gillberg even mention a 30% increase of carbon dioxide outlet, compared to today, because of the presumably higher traffic rate

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Looking at it this way, this is something that do not seem to correspond that well with the title of their article

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Yet another aspect that would shed new light over this issue, not taken into account by the authors, is the proposed increase of travelling from outside the Öresund region, by people heading for the bridge

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: When the psychological obstacle of, perhaps, paying extra on the ferry for bringing a car than not, the inconvenience of having to match time-tables, cancelled trips because of weather etc

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: disappear, it is very likely that more people than today spontaneously cross the sound

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: This, by all means, is the aim of the bridge, to increase the accessibility to both regions, but scarcely of benefit for the environment

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: An increased amount of emissions is thus also to be expected outside the area of Skåne

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: A conclusion that is drawn in the article, is that the fare for crossing the bridge should be lowered substantially compared to plans, to make an increase of traffic possible

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Taking into consideration what have been mentioned above, this would not be of benefit for the environment neither in the region of Öresund, nor in the regions further away from the actual crossing point

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Something which however would cause a positive effect on the environment, not even mentioned in the article, is lowered fares on trains crossing the bridge, generous, well-planned timetables, good connections with other public transport etc

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: This would in fact mean, easy accessibility from all parts of the countries surrounding Öresund, a minimum of pollution both to the regions further away from and in the Öresund area, and an elimination of emissions both from the ferry service and car traffic on the actual bridge and in its surroundings

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: Conclusively one could argue that this article is narrow-minded by means of considering increased car traffic as the only way of improving exchange between regions

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: However with this starting-point, the authors of course in several ways try to argue in favour of the positive effects of an increased amount of traffic by car

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: What makes their argumentation insufficient, and untrustworthy in spite of the statistics pointing in a positive direction, is however that they amongst other things 1

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: compare tomorrow's technique in car-production to today's of ferry-production, 2

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: do not mention that even a lowered amount of pollution still have negative effects on the environment, especially since they have neglected the effect on areas outside the Öresund region, and 3

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: do not take into consideration less pollutant means of travelling, for example trains

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
text: I can consequently not find that an increased amount of car traffic would have a positive effect on the environment after reading the article

title: Can increased car traffic have a positive effect on the environment?
The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: In "The Temptations of the Basque" (Deia sept

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: 6, 1999), manuel de Unciti argues that the nationalist sector in Euskadi does not help Basque people reach a complete pacification there

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: He explains that the nationalist political parties would really like to set up a conciliatory dialogue between them and the central government in Madrid, but the truth is that they want to be in control of that dialogue and also impose the basic conditions for it

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Moreover, the central government is doing its best to be in contact with both parts but it is the nationalist that refuse to the approach from Madrid

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Unciti states that the independence of the Basque Country from Spain is now something unreachable, a "utopia"

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: For him, it suggests an alternative nationalist ideology based mainly on the following motto: "You cannot obtain anything without violence"

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Finally, Unciti concludes that the nationalist refuse to say what they believe in publicly and he scarcely considers it possible to extend the supreme fatherland of Euskadi from its current borders to other areas such as Navarre, La Rioja and some southern provinces in France

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Essay arguing against the article: Everybody agrees that "The Basque problem" has always been an untouchable question for many people living in Spain

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Now that weapons are in silence and that there are not terrorist acts any more, it seems that people have suddenly started to talk freely and give their own opinions about this problem, mainly about a final pacification in the Basque Country

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: The absence of corpses on the pavements makes it possible for us to state our own beliefs not so cautiously as before, but with a great freedom longed so much by the Basque society

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It is obvious that not everybody agrees with the rest of the people, but there are always many divergences

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Above all, when we deal with the idea of independence and the right of self-determination for the Basque Country

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Manuel de Unciti, a famous priest and journalist, does not support this idea and he blames the nationalist for not being able to set up a dialogue between them and the central government in Madrid

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: My aim in this article is to argue against this and other opinions of him and also state my own

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: To begin with, I really think that it is likely to establish an encouraging dialogue between the two parts that will lead everything to the right road

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: No blame attaches to the Basque nationalists when they are accused of not bringing this dialogue to a successful conclusion

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: , but it is the central government who actually must shoulder the responsibilities

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It does not show signs of raising "the Basque question" in its most radical causes and it keeps being stubborn with the idea that nobody can put a high price to peace

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: The current Spanish government led by José María Aznar, resists to accept that the matter pending between Euskadi and the rest of Spain is mainly of a political nature

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: All initiatives of advance from the central government, praised so much by many mass media in Madrid, prove ineffective

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: In a way, they sabotage the process of peace in the Basque Country since Aznar does not want to change the penitentiary politics and absolutely refuses to build up a meeting forum without exclusions

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Even the violent ones (those who committed terrorist acts in the past) must be there

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Everybody has to listen to what the others believe in and the political dialogue has to be carried out without any kind of pre-conditions

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Secondly, I believe that independence from Spain is not a utopia, as Unciti asserts

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: On the contrary, it is something feasible

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: We, the Basque, constitute a single nation

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: We have our own culture, our own customs and above all, our own language (Euskera) completely different from the rest of the languages which are spoken in Spain

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Thus, we should aspire to achieve a cultural unity

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It is that the current European integrating political movements make independence from Spain difficult but at least, it would be possible for us to obtain the right of self-determination as a country including our own legislation in every field

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: In addition, I do not want here to take a radical and unreasonable attitude against what is Spanish

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: I accept that we still have many bonds with Spain, but it is obvious that we must defend what is ours

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Independence will probably bring a final pacification to the Basque Country

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: A pluralist society will be established and the people living in it will live in harmony

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Everybody will listen to what the others think and so, will accept all different opinions

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: What is more, violence will finish

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It is once again a responsibility of the central government in Madrid to put an end to this situation that divides a whole nation and also puts the two parts living together face to face

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Lastly and closely related to the idea of independence is the idea of a supreme fatherland for Euskadi

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: The latter has to be extended from its current borders to other areas such as Navarre, some southern provinces of France, La Rioja and Huesca

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Unciti refuses to accept this suggestion

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: He says it is "an stimulating dream" of the Basque and he explains that few people believe this possible, In a way it is, though there are, of course, many difficulties

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It is said that France will never give any province or territory from its fatherland to the Basque and besides, it is backed up by the European Community

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: It is also true that there is a minimal part of the population that cherishes independence in those French provinces, but I personally feel that a referendum should be done to know the opinion the population

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: If we look back on the past, we see that the old Basque Country consisted of all the previous provinces together with the current ones (Bizkaia, Gipuzkoa and Araba) which were linked by their own customs and language

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Basque nationalists do not use demagogy as a political means or weapon, but they only try to bring up a future of grandeur for the Basque Country and its people

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: In conclusion, it is the great majority of the Basque people that wishes to work hard for a definite pacification in Euskadi

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: We are living a historical moment of peace right now and we must keep it forever

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Everybody has to respect his opponent and make it possible to grant all the cessions needed

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: Now, pacification requires the establishment of a dialogue without pre-conditions and without exclusions

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
text: I am fully convinced that, with the passage of time and without violence, everything will be firmly put in its right place and problems will be given their true weight to discuss them more objectively

title: The temptations of the Basque: A Revised Summary.
Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: In an article in the Dagens Nyheter 00 03 01 representatives of the National Agency for Government Employers argue that a legislation concerning a shortening of the working hours will harm the state economy

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I believe that they are wrong

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: In modern society, more and more people are becoming overworked and consequently, sick leave and early retirement related to stress are increasing

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I argue that a shortening of the working week to 35 hours would help decrease these problems

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: In this essay, I will show that this legislation has the ability to pay for itself

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I will start by showing how it will affect the working population and continue by showing how the state will benefit from it

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I will also show how it can be financed without harming the state or the individuals concerned

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Sweden's unstable economy with increasing unemployment and major cuts on welfare has made the workload heavier for many people

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Fewer people are supposed to do the same amount of work as before and they feel that they have to accomplish even more to be sure of keeping their jobs

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: This has been a strong contributor to the increase of stress-related-illnesses during the last few years

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Today, more than 250

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: 000 people are on psychopharmacological drugs2 and people take sick leave due to stress

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I am not suggesting that shorter working days would solve these problems but it would surely lower the pressure

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: People will get more time to spend with family and friends and more time to relax, they will not feel as stressed out and can thereby be more effective at work

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Furthermore, many presently unemployed can get jobs and through that, more stimulating lives

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: However, I do not think that the National Agency for Government Employers disagree with me on these points

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Their main concern is the financing i

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: e

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: how much this law would cost the state and the employers

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: In the article they claim that it would be vary expensive and that it has to be paid either by lowering or by making major cuts in the public sector

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: I argue that this will not be necessary

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: The initial costs can be financed by the state using the current budget surplus

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: However, in the long run I am convinced that a general shortening of the working hours will pay for itself, both by decreasing the state's expenses and by increasing its income

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: The state will not have to spend as much money on healthcare and medicine for overworked people

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Furthermore, fewer people would depend on state benefits, such as sickness-, unemployment- and supplementary benefit

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: The fact that people are employed has a positive effect on the national economy in several ways

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Firstly it saves money otherwise spent on benefits and secondly people who work pay taxes and they also tend to spend more money, which is important in order to keep the economy in good shape

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Furthermore I argue that everyone; employers, employees and the state, will benefit from shorter working weeks, since the employees will have more energy and consequently be more effective the hours they do work

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: 1 Arbetsgivarverket (vet ej hur jag ska översätta) 2 Holger Eriksson et al

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: "Staten har råd att korta arbetstiden" DN 00 02 20 Summary of the article: "Kortad arbetstid skadar staten" (DN 00 03 01) The National Agency for Government Employers1 in Sweden says no to legislation about a 35-hour working week

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: They argue that such a law would hurt the state as well as the population and say that this is a question that should be solved within the individual workplaces

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: According to them a general shortening of the working-hours would be very expensive and would have to be financed either by lower salaries or by major cuts in the public sector

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: They also believe that shorter workdays would lead to recruitment problems, especially within the public sector where there is already a shortage of staff

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
text: Their conclusion is that there is no way that this legislation could come through without severe damages to the state economy

title: Everyone Will Benefit from a Shorter Working Week.
Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Recently, there has been a debate going on in the UNT about homosexuals' right to adopt children

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: In this essay I will argue against a number of articles written by people who are against adoption for homosexual couples

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: I am well aware of the strong opposition against homosexuals' right to adopt that exists in today's fairly liberal society

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: It is a very sensitive question, also in the younger generations

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: In fact, it is a question that crosses the borders of age, social class and background; whatever group you are a part of, it is not obvious what your opinion in this question will be

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: This makes the topic even more interesting and my task to argue for the legalization of homosexuals' right to adopt even more challenging

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: What strikes me when reading these articles against homosexuals' right to adopt is the ignorance and prejudice of some of the authors

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: One man talks about homosexuals and lesbians as two different things, meaning that only men are homosexuals and women are lesbians

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Of course, a well-informed person would know that the word "homosexual" applies to both men and women

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Another person argues that if you have "chosen" to live in a homosexual relationship, you have also lost the right to have children

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Who has ever heard of a homosexual or a heterosexual who has "chosen" their sexuality? In one of the articles, a fear is expressed about the possibility that the child might grow up as a homosexual itself, if raised in a homosexual family

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: First of all, there are no examples that confirm such a theory; homosexuality is not some kind of contagious disease

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: But secondly, and more important, why would that possibility (if there were one) be something to fear? Most probable, this fear is something that is based on prejudice and ignorance

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: One argument taken up in several of these articles is the importance of keeping the nuclear family intact, that the children need both a mother and a father

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: This argument seems rather weak to me, thinking about how common it is in today's society that children grow up with only a mother, or a father

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Is it not better that the child has two adults to turn to than only one, regardless of sex and sexuality? The words "it is unnatural" is often used

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: But why should it be less natural for two men to raise a child than a single woman? Homosexuality has always existed, it is something natural, and it is about time we start considering it as natural

title: Homosexuals' Right to Adopt: Why should it be legalized?
text: Another argument is that the child can not choose parents and that it would be illiberal to force the child into something it is not capable to judge in yet, especially since a common fear is that the child is at risk of being teased by other children for their homosexual parents
