title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: In the Dagens Nyheter 99-09-27 there was an article that argued against homosexual couples having the right to adopt children

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: The arguments that were used concentrated on what was best for the child that was to be adopted, a viewpoint I completely agree with, but I found their arguments to be invalid

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Firstly, that children shouldn't be adopted since they will have difficulties in their lives, if adopted by homosexual parents

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Secondly, that legitimate adoptions to Sweden might decrease since the majority of the biological mothers or representatives for orphans don't want to give their child up for adoption to homosexual couples

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Thirdly that there should be no more experimenting with the lives of children that are adopted

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: These arguments might seem valid when out of the context, but I will argue against this article in order to show that homosexual couples are not less suitable to be parents than heterosexual couples are

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: To begin with, it is argued that it is not right to let homosexual couples adopt children, since these children would go through hard times

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: It is not specified in the article what kind of problems and obstacles these children would encounter, but I believe that this argument concerns problems in society, such as mobbing or other kind of negative responses from society regarding the configuration of the family of the child

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Nevertheless, every child can face hard times and problems in every kind of family, it doesn't have to be a family where the parents are homosexual

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Joakim Anrell writes in the Internet magazine that it is an exaggeration to blame all the problems in a family on the fact that the parents are homosexual

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: He also points out that there is nothing that indicates that problems, when there are some, are caused by the child's parents being homosexual and not by some other factor

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Furthermore, if it is in the child's best interest that society shouldn't put it in a situation where it can go through hard times how can then the adoption of coloured children be justified? They too can go through hard times and even be harassed, since there are racist opinions in Sweden today

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Since it is clear that it is not only children that live with homosexual parents that can go through hard times, how can it then be used as an argument against adoption by homosexual parents? The second argument concerns what might happen if homosexual couples would be allowed to adopt children

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: It is argued that the chances of legitimate international adoptions to Sweden might decrease, since homosexual couples are not accepted as parents by the countries that give up children for adoption and the majority of the mothers or representatives for orphans don't want to give a child up for adoption by homosexual couples

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: It is important to keep up good relations with other countries, but it is equally important not to discriminate a minority that can and want's to help take care of the less fortunate children of the world

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: A solution to this problem, to both prevent a possible decrease in international adoptions and see too the best of the children by providing them with a good home and a loving family, could perhaps be accomplished by allowing homosexual couples a limited right to adopt children

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: This could be done in such a way that the persons that give up a child for adoption may decide whether they want the child to be adopted by hetero- or homosexual parents

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: The number of children given up for adoption by homosexual couples would of course be limited, but this way a few more children could get a good home

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Perhaps the limitation wont prevent the countries from stopping adoptions to Sweden, but it would be an acceptable compromise, at least for the time being

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: This limitation could later be removed when society has accepted families that consist of homosexual parents

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: The third and final argument states that there should be no more experimenting with the lives of the children that are adopted

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Since the argument isn't further developed it is hard to see what is meant by it

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: I assume that giving a child homosexual parents is what in this case is meant by experimenting

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: In a motion to the Swedish Parliament Andreas Carlgren writes that in a study conducted by the university of Gothenburg concerning children growing up with homosexual parents concluded that it is not the configuration of the family which affects the child most, instead it is how the children are raised and what kind of support they receive in society

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: This argument also suggests that it has been accepted to experiment with heterosexual parents - These have all been different individuals who have been investigated thoroughly before being allowed to adopt a child

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: There is no way of knowing what happened to those heterosexual families after a few years, perhaps they couldn't take care of the child properly or perhaps they became abusive, still the child and the parents were given a chance

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Homosexuals are not even allowed to apply for adoption and be examined and I believe that that right should apply to everyone

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Only this way it is possible to find the best possible parents to a child

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: In conclusion, homosexual couples should be allowed to adopt children but that right should perhaps be limited in the beginning

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Every child in every kind of family might go through hard times and this argument can therefore not be used against homosexual couples

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Decreasing chances to adopt children also for heterosexuals could be the result if homosexual couples would be allowed to adopt children

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: That is a risk society should be willing to take

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: There are mothers that agree to their child being adopted by homosexuals and letting these children live with to people of the same sex wouldn't be to experiment with their lives

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: A study made by the university of Gothenburg shows that the constellation of the family is less important than how the child is raised and how it is supported by society

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: The right to apply for adoption and be examined should apply to everyone

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: If the homosexual couple is denied an adoption because their financial situation isn't satisfactory, it would be acceptable

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: If they are denied an adoption because their home doesn't look suitable for a child to live in, that too would be acceptable

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: It would not be acceptable to deny them the chance of adopting a child simply because they are homosexual

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: In an article in the Dagens Nyheter 99-11-27, Adoptivbarns rätt inte homofobi, it was argued why homosexual couples mustn't be allowed to adopt children

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: What is best for the child is what is most important, it is also important for the child to know later in life that the adoption was carried out in an proper way

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: International adoptions to Sweden might stop if a law was introduced, granting homosexual couples the right to adopt children, since many countries don't want to give children up for adoption by homosexuals

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: The right for homosexual couples to adopt children internationally would have to be preceeded by working for the homosexuals' rights in other parts of the world

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
text: Adoptivbarns rätt inte homofobi I: Dagens Nyheter 99-09-27 Anrell, Joakim, Adoptivbarnens rätt till trygghet magazine 99-10-26 Carlgren, Andreas, 1997/98:L424 Motion till riksdagen

title: Let homosexual couples adopt children!
Yes or no to women firemen
text: In the article "Do not lower the standards for women" in Göteborgs Posten, from March 14th 2000, Christer Janson, a fireman, gives his opinion about whether there should be different tests for men and women applying for rescue forces

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He claims that the most important qualities that a fireman needs are strength and stamina

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Thereafter come technical and tactical skills

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Janson asserts that the rescue forces in Sweden can only afford to have the best staff because they are not as many as they are in other countries

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He also thinks that both men and women should go through the same kind of tests in order to preserve the high standard that rescue forces have today

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Otherwise, he claims, their quality would be seriously damaged

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Janson also suggests that women who want to work within rescue forces should have a different kind of duties, which are better suited to their physical capacities

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: As I mentioned in the introduction, Christer Janson claims in his article that men and women cannot possibly do an equally good job as firemen

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: The argument against women working in rescue forces that he stresses most is that women do not have same physical strength and stamina as men

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Thereby, they cannot do this kind of job as well as men can

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He mentions that the equipment that firemen use has been better than in the past, but that there is still a demand for a lighter equipment

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He sees this demand only as a way to make it easier for women to work as firemen

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: What he does not seem to realise is that lighter equipment would make work easier for all firemen, not only for female ones

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He also does not think that there should not be older firemen, although older ones cannot be as strong as younger ones

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: It is difficult not to argue that there cannot be a big difference between an older male fireman and a female one when it comes to the issue of strength and stamina

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: This argument of his that women should not become firemen because they do not have physical strength is based on the prejudices that all women are weak

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: There are, in fact, many strong women as well as many with staying power

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Besides strength and stamina, Christer Janson also stresses the importance of technical and tactical skills

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: The argument against female firemen is even more difficult to accept, because these skills have nothing to do with a person's sex, but are results of education and experience gathered during a person's career

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: To claim that male firemen have better technical and tactical skills is the same as to claim that they have easier to learn than female firemen

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Nevertheless, in Janson's opinion the most natural and effective thing in order to have an ideal working team would be to use a young, healthy and storng male fireman (he would stand for Janson's demand for strength and stamina) together with an older and an experienced partner (they would then together stand for technical and tactical skills)

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: In a way, Christer Janson gives a counterargument to his own argument about the necessity of strength for a fireman

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: If there should be a young and strong man in a rescue team, than it means that the rest of it is not strong enough, though it consists of males only

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: In that case it should not matter, not even for Janson, if one of the other two would be a woman

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Still, Janson has more arguments against women firemen

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: His most astonishing argument is that with women firemen "

title: Yes or no to women firemen
Yes or no to women firemen
Yes or no to women firemen
text: the knowledge and experience is going to disappear

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: " He asserts that this would do to the fact that women do not take part in rescue actions as long as men and consequently they are not able to build up knowledge and experience

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: This argument is based on his own belief that all women firemen behave in the same way and have exactly same way of life

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: However, in this profession, as well as in any other, the development of a person's career is highly individual

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Besides, there are quite surely many male firemen who quit there careers before the retirement

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: Consequently, if there were more women firemen, then there would certainly be a higher per cent of them to take part in rescue actions during a long period of time

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: In his article, Christer Janson presents different arguments against women firemen

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: He does not only claim that men and women should take the same kind of test in order to be firemen, but it is obvious from the text that he does not agree with the idea that women are able to successfully work in a profession of this kind

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: His arguments are, among others, that women are not strong enough, that they do not have enough technical and tactical skills because they do not stay within rescue forces long enough

title: Yes or no to women firemen
text: His arguments are vague and indicate a disbelief in not only women's abilities as such, but even in their ability to collaborate with men

title: Yes or no to women firemen
About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: In Sweden we all remember the images of the confrontations between the police and stone throwing, masked demonstrators, and the vast demolishing of public and private property

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: We have all wondered what it was all for, what went wrong, and why it happened

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: In an article named About Violence and "Violence", Staffan Jacobson gives his views on the latter, and in a sense gives his approval to what happened, claiming that the "police state" and the world situation are the sole culpables

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: There are many things in Jacobson's article that are of dubious factual character, and I would like to begin by correcting one of them

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: He claims that one of the main reasons behind the anger displayed by the violent demonstrations in Gothenburg is that the Swedish people have been forced into EU, "the union of the rich" as he calls it, against the will of the majority

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Need I remind him that we held a popular vote about Sweden entering into EU? The figures were 52

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: 3 percent for, and 46

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: 8 percent against (with 0

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: 9 percent being blank votes)

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: I leave it as an exercise to the reader to draw his or her own conclusions on this

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Another aspect I am amazed at is his strong, almost vehement, aversity toward the police, and his claims that having a police force is a step away from democracy

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Perhaps we would not need the police if we lived in a perfect world, but the fact is that we really do need some kind of organization to keep criminal, violent, and dangerous elements in society at bay

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: The police have not always made the right decisions, or made the right thing, but would we be better off without them? They are a necessary evil; a small restriction that we need to keep democracy working

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: They need to be checked by other instances in society, but in my opinion they are part of a practical, working democracy, not a step away from it

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: These two issues aside, Jacobson turns his sights on what he calls the devaluation of the word "violence" that is being made by the common, "bourgeois" press

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: This is a central theme in his article

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: He gives his own definition of what violence really is, naming, among others, the Israeli occupation of Palestine, starvation in third world countries versus the fattening of the industrial nations, etc

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: What irks me is his last statement on the list: It is violence when the global capitalism humiliate, torment, and profit on us all for a miserable pay, or no pay at all; destroy our common lives with alienation, emptiness, and hopelessness

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: What world is Jacobson living in? What we do with our lives is our own choice

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: It is not governed by some grand, capitalistic, overarching hand

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Our will is our own

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Granted, multinational companies have power, but that power has been given to them by us, the consumers

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: If he had been talking about third world countries his arguments would have carried more weight, but he isn't

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: The thing is, no one is saying that the things he lists as "violence" are not bad things, or that our world does not need improvement

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Not even the common press is saying that

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: In fact, would we even be aware of the worldwide situation he describes if it were not for the media? All his arguments in the article lead to a single, final statement: that the violent demonstrators in Gothenburg and Genoa were justified in their displayed anger, and that this display was even a positive one

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: What is that violence, compared to the global injustices that happen every day? My answer to that is that if Jacobson is so concerned about democracy, he should take a closer, more critical look at the methodologies of these shrouded activists

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Either they work within the framework of democracy by demonstrating and debating peacefully, or they work outside it

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: By using violence these groups have gone the way of terrorism and nihilism, and are therefore working against Jacobson's treasured democracy

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: His argument seems to be that the lesser evil has a justified existence simply because the greater evil exists

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: I say that that is like comparing apples to oranges, and, using his own words; devaluates the word "violence"

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: Summary of the article About Violence and "Violence" In an article in SvD, August 2, Staffan Jacobson argues that the violent demonstrations in Gothenburg, and later in Genoa, were the justified, positive results of frustration emanating from the global misuse of power by the capitalistic forces in society

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
text: The vessel to his arguments is the devaluation of the word "violence" made by the common press

title: About Violence and "Violence", a Counter Argument
Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: After reading an article written by Arne Helldén about the new suggestion how to change the teacher training program in Sweden I would like to respond to a few things

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The basic idea in the suggested change is to shorten the education for future teachers and therefore also change the amount of subject studies and the time for practical experience

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: Instead of a deep knowledge in a few subjects there will be less specialised knowledge in many subjects and less time to practice as a teacher together with a class

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: I do not agree with this and that is what I am going to discuss

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: I think that practical experience is very important and it would be a mistake to shorten it by half as suggested

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: I also believe that a less specialised knowledge in the subjects you are teaching in will make it harder to teach it to pupils

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The specialisation in the subjects you as a teacher are going to teach is to be shortened according to this new suggested change

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: I do not believe that this change could be done without serious consequences

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: In Sweden today the students on the teachers learning program read one and a half-year of each of their chosen subjects (often two subjects)

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The new suggestion will obviously give us future teachers less knowledge and therefore less chance of learning our pupils something

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: A deep understanding for a subject is extremely important when you are supposed to explain it to someone else

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: If you cannot see the whole picture it is very hard to describe the smaller parts of the problem and also to understand why someone does not understand it

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: A very common question among pupils is "why do we have to learn this?" When a teacher get that question I think it is important to be able to explain the issue from a greater aspect and show the pupils that the things they are learning will be useful for them the rest of their lives

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: One more important issue of this is that the more you know about something the more self-secure you feel and if pupils can feel that you are secure about yourself the more they trust and listen to you

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: Another way of getting that self-confidence is through experience

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: This new compact version of the teacher training program does not only contain less specialising it also contains less practical experience

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The new idea is that future teachers only have to be out in a real school environment as teachers ten weeks all together during the whole period of education

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: Society is constantly changing and so are the schools

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: A person that went to school a few years ago does not really know what it is like to go to school today

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: Therefore, it is very important to go out and see what it is that is expected of you when you come out as a teacher for the first time

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: It is also very valuable to try the role as a teacher while someone is watching you and giving constructive criticism

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: A real classroom situation can never be simulated is a correct way

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: You can never foretell how pupils are going to react to something you say or do

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: It is also hard to know how you will react in certain situations and what would be the best solution to a problem

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: I do not say that twenty weeks of practical experience will give you all the answers but at least it will prepare you for some of it and help you become the kind of teacher you want to be

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: Experience, knowledge and a good self-confidence are very important when it comes to being a good teacher

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: All these three things hang together and effect each other

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The goal must always be to become the kind of teacher who can bring about knowledge to pupils

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: If you take away the knowledge from a teacher there will be nothing to teach

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: If you instead take away the experience or the self-confidence the teaching will not be as good as it could and ought to be

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: You cannot remove one piece and expect to maintain the same standard as before

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: The teachers that are teaching in schools in Sweden today have specialised in a few subjects and therefore have a deep knowledge in that special area

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
text: If the suggestion goes through there will not be the same depth of knowledge and I think that that will lower the standard in our schools

title: Winds of Change on Teacher Training Program
Essay Task 2
text: We are all experts in questions that concern schooling since we all have personal experiences about it

title: Essay Task 2
text: I think it is good with lots of ideas, the latest I have come across is from Sweden where they are planning to cut down hours for teacher trainees in traditional subjects and teach these trainees more in non school subjects

title: Essay Task 2
text: The idea is not to transform knowledge to pupils but to make them search knowledge, this is the impression I got from Arne Helldén's article in Dagens Nyheter(May2000)

title: Essay Task 2
text: If we take this idea outside Sweden and see what is of general interest, I think the first thing that comes to mind is how this would effect the learning process of our children

title: Essay Task 2
text: I think the best thing with this idea is that it would activate pupils to search for knowledge instead of sitting and waiting the teacher to give them the "right" answers

title: Essay Task 2
text: It would also make pupils to learn the stuff much better because it would be the result of their own work, they would learn for life and not for exams

title: Essay Task 2
text: This is a way learning that has the idea to improve person

title: Essay Task 2
text: The problem with this new idea is that it takes such a long time to search relevant information

title: Essay Task 2
text: Being able to see what is relevant and what is not relevant is a very valuable skill and I think it should be trained in school but how do you know what is relevant, one cannot expect a teenager to know how to do it

title: Essay Task 2
text: It is here the teacher comes in, even in this new system

title: Essay Task 2
text: I think it should be the teacher who evaluates the relevancy of pupils knowledge

title: Essay Task 2
text: Otherwise there may be pupils in geography lessons who know the exact addresses of every Spice Girls member but cannot name the Nordic countries

title: Essay Task 2
text: The only way for a teacher to see what is relevant is the fact that she or he has sufficient command of the subject and I think there is a risk that teachers do not know enough about their subject which they teach if one reduces schooling of traditional subjects in teacher trainee programs

title: Essay Task 2
text: Where as the new system emphasizes the individual, the old system triumphs the society

title: Essay Task 2
text: The old system gives some basic and simple knowledge of things

title: Essay Task 2
text: Things are not been complicated too much, it makes the teacher job much easier but at the same time it is very boring for the pupils

title: Essay Task 2
text: They are only expected to know what the books say

title: Essay Task 2
text: How ever boring it may seem and feel there is a point in it

title: Essay Task 2
text: Since we all cannot become overpaid internet designers or other philosophers we do not have to complicate things with thinking alternative options

title: Essay Task 2
text: So in this sense our school systems do work for separating people to truck drivers and doctors, there has to be a way to do this and the old system shows how hard one has to work for a nice work

title: Essay Task 2
text: In the new system the learning cannot be compared so easily because learning is more individual

title: Essay Task 2
text: As I see it, one of the main tasks schools have is to make us useful for the society and this I think the old system manages to do better

title: Essay Task 2
text: I have to admit that this kind of divining people into groups in school seems very cruel, especially when we all learn in our own speed, which the old system does not take into counts

title: Essay Task 2
text: But on the other hand it is also preparing for the adult life and job-seeking

title: Essay Task 2
text: Both methods seem to have very clear positive effects

title: Essay Task 2
text: It would be ideal situation if one could combine the best sides of both

title: Essay Task 2
text: This would mean that we would get pupils who have a sense that although absolute knowledge is rare, there is still some knowledge one needs

title: Essay Task 2
text: Questioning and analyzing attitude together with a sense of finding the essential knowledge out of mass of knowledge that is important

title: Essay Task 2
text: This is especially important if we think the world we live in, internet is full of information, the difficulty is to know what is reliable information

title: Essay Task 2
text: This kind of knowledge is very useful for every pupil, no matter what their future plans are

title: Essay Task 2
text: I think teachers are the right persons to learn pupils how to find this golden path to the greater knowledge but she or he needs a real education and not some strange subjects

title: Essay Task 2
text: Teachers have to have enough knowledge in order to lead her or his pupils in this path of learning

title: Essay Task 2
text: I would like to see a school where teachers have good knowledge of things but are still capable to encourage their pupils to search for knowledge with in limits

title: Essay Task 2

Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: A phenomenon which is spreading rapidly is the notion of being burnt out

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The term burnt out is used for people who are exhausted due to too much work and stress, and who simply cannot continue in the same pace as they have been doing

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The author of the article that I am going to argue against claims that: "being burnt out is just being slack"

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The title of the article is: "Being burnt out is just being slack", and the author simply states this even though not a shred of evidence is presented to support this statement

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: There is no explanation whatsoever in the article showing in what way this phenomenon indicates laziness

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Instead, the author takes up several irrelevant issues in this lame attempt to make a statement

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The author begins by implying that almost everyone goes around feeling a bit burnt out, and make it sound as if people see this phenomenon as a chance of escaping work

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: It is obvious that no one wants to be unhappy, feel bad and not be able to attend work, and claiming that that may be the case is just a loose accusation which is entirely untrue

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The fact that all those people who are unemployed feel so frustrated is a good example which proves that people do not enjoy being idle

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Secondly it is argued that the term "burnt out" indicates something irrevocable, and therefore it is not a suitable name for this phenomenon

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: It should rather be called "being over worked"

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Being burnt out is actually something irrevocable because someone who has been burnt out once is not likely to be able to go back to the same tasks as before

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The fact that you are burnt out indicates that you have to slow down or you will become severely ill

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Furthermore, it is argued that this phenomenon was former treated with a few weeks in a rest home and that was that, whereas today those who are burnt out wander about in find yourself courses

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: It should rather be argued that those people who were sent away to rest homes due to overwork are most likely the same people who are now burnt out

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: If they would have got the proper treatment in the first place, instead of just being sent away a few weeks, they would not be burnt out today

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: In addition to this argument the author also states that: "they were earlier sent away to rest homes, but now their find yourself courses are financed by taxpayers

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: This statement indicates that there should be a difference in financing rest homes and find yourself courses, which there is not

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Where does the author suppose the money for the former came from if not from the tax payers, and since it seems to be okay to use tax money to pay for rest homes, why is it not okay to finance other treatments as well? Another argument is that "ordinary people have to work twice as hard to make a living" which seems to imply that the author believes that the taxes have been dramatically raised during the last years, with the sole purpose of financing all these burnt out people

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: This is obviously not the case, but pure fiction

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: We should rather be thankful that there is a safety net in Sweden financed by tax money that provides alternatives to losing one's job, which will be the result if burnt out people are not offered treatment

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: It is further stated that those who are being burnt out are simply taking advantage of the system

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: I would say that they are not using the system in a bad way, which the author suggests, but they are using it the way it was designed to be used

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: A person who is stressed and not feeling well has every right to make use of a system created for that purpose

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: Finally, those who are burnt out are exhorted to "clench their teeth and go to work like the rest of us on Monday mornings"

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: This statement indicates a great lack of understanding and ignorance, and it is clear that the person who wrote this article is very frustrated due to people who are burnt out

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: The author is driven by his/her personal prejudices and not by presenting true facts

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
text: People are being burnt out due to the increased amount of stress in the society, not due to a will to escape work

title: Does being burnt out equals being slack?
Long Live the school of knowledge
text: In order to respond the demands of the society of today, where various shifts and changes take place at a rapid pace, the Swedish Teachers' Education Committee (LUK), which mostly consists of people with no practical experience from the school, has proposed a restructuring of the Swedish teachers' education programs

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: In short, the idea of the proposition is to reduce the studies of the special subjects, and devote that time on other issues that the Teachers' Education Committee regards as more crucial and valuable

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: This proposal has given rise to a fairly large debate in Swedish media, and not in vain

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: As a future teacher I don't see a point in reducing the subject studies, but in fact I consider the reduction to be a great threat that would lead to a radical lowering of the teachers' competence and moreover the standard of the basic training at Swedish schools

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: The idea, which the plan of the restructuring is based on, is that the teachers would no more have the intermediary role in the classroom, but they would be there to guide the pupils to find their own knowledge

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: Therefore the Teachers' Education Committee thinks that it's unnecessary to have as much subject education for the future teachers as they have at the moment, but half the subject studies would be enough

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: Instead the teacher students would devote themselves to issues such as how the knowledge is formed and how it can be organized

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: As senior lecturer and teacher trainer Arne Helldén from Linköping expresses in his article in Dagens Nyheter (May 10, 2000), the proposal appears very ill-defined and vague

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: School is still a place where pupils are supposed to learn things and develop themselves, isn't it? How can they learn anything if there is nobody to convey the knowledge to them? The view of the teachers being guides while the pupils explore and search out the knowledge by themselves certainly looks nice on a paper and sounds good in theory, but in practice it would lead to a catastrophe

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: There are too many pupils who are not ready to take the enormous responsibility for their own learning which the Teachers' Education Committee's suggestion requires

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: The pupils need a person, a teacher who conveys the basic knowledge that lays the foundation of their knowledge development, and creates opportunities for their further education

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: To be able to act as an intermediary, teachers will definitely need to know how to teach

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: However, it doesn't help to be professional in knowing how to teach without having something to teach

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: To half the subject education would drastically decrease teachers' competence, and make them incapable to fulfil the most important duty of a teacher as an "agent" between the pupils and the knowledge

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: Both the thorough knowledge of the subject and the know-how of teaching are important parts of the teachers' competence

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: The other should not exclude the other one, but a balance between them should be found

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: Teachers' education programs have in general a difficult duty to adjust to the changes that go on both in and outside of the school

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: At the same time the programs have an extremely important role in changing the teacher profession and thus developing the school

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: While weighing the proposal of the restructuring of the teachers' education, we should keep in mind from which quarter it comes from

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
text: We shouldn't make changes that have such disastrous consequences head over heels, and above all, the biggest voice should be given those who then afterwards should put the ideas into practice

title: Long Live the school of knowledge
An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: For quite a while it has been debated whether Sweden should remain a monarchy or become a republic

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Republicans mean that the monarchy is an old-fashioned state of government which there is not room for in a modern democracy as Sweden

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: They argue against the fact that the Head of State is not elected by the people according to his or her competence

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Below I intend to explain why these arguments need to be reconsidered

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: As reference I will use an article on the issue by Rasmus Jonlund of the Republican Society in Stockholm

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Jonlund's main concern is that the monarchy is a threat towards the democracy of Sweden

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: He states that the monarch is Head of State, and the highest office of the country should, according to democratic values, be elected by the people

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: This is true, but Jonlund makes the monarch appear as the most powerful person of the country

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: He does not consider that the king or queen only has representational duties of importance today

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: I argue that the actual power lies with the Government and voters choose which parties should be represented there

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Indeed, the monarch is the chairperson of several committees and boards within the Government, a reminiscence from older days when the monarch's power was stronger, but in reality, the power of the people is not at all affected by this

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Secondly, Jonlund wants to abolish the monarchy with consideration for the royalties' own good

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Of course I agree with him in this respect: media is often going too far, with journalists penetrating into crown princess Victoria's private life and so on

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: But will there be a change overnight just by introducing a republican system of government? Tabloids and similar press has gained such popularity and has become such a large part of media nowadays

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: The truth is that the private lives of official persons will always be of interest

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Even if there is not a monarch as Head of State, there will be someone else whose life - together with his or her family's life - will be speculated on and written about

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Just look at the Clinton family! The President's wife and daughter are well-known in media, and of what interest are they to ordinary people? What I want to say is that famous people always will be a prey of journalists and photographers

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Thus the argument that Sweden should abolish the monarchy to save people's privacy is not valid

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: It will not work

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Rasmus Jonlund's third argument concerns the competence of the Head of State

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: He holds that the monarch is not elected according to his or her qualifications

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Once again I think that Jonlund is confusing the role of the monarch

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: The prime minister is chosen carefully and would never be appointed unless he or she was found suitable

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: The importance in competence lies with the prime minister

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: The monarch is born into his office as Head of State and is raised and educated as such, growing into the role

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Therefore I argue that the two hold completely different offices in society and should not be compared

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Jonlund and his fellow republicans see the question about the monarchy in Sweden as a most burning issue

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Myself I feel that much of the debate is exaggerated

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: People are not suffering from living in a monarchy, so why change state of government? It will not solve any problems

title: An exaggerated debate - the monarchy does not harm Sweden!
text: Neither is it worth the time and effort required!

title: Swedish education in danger
text: In Sweden the Teachers' Educational Committee (LUK) has proposed a reform of the Swedish teacher's education programs and the Swedish parliament is soon to decide the future of Swedish school

title: Swedish education in danger
text: The reform contains quiet drastic changes and has been intensively debated in Swedish media

title: Swedish education in danger
text: One of the bigger changes is that less time will be spent on subject studies, an reduction from 120 academic points to only 40 possibly 60 academic points

title: Swedish education in danger
text: In Dagens Nyheter Arne Helldén, lecturer at Linköping University, gives the reader the main features of the reform and strongly argues against it

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Unfortunately the picture of what LUK's reform consist of does not get very clear when Helldén is trying to explain the reform's main features

title: Swedish education in danger
text: However I will as well as Helldén, and in support of his article, argue against LUK's proposal by explaining the absurdity of the reform and by arguing for the importance of having the present amount of subject studies

title: Swedish education in danger
text: LUK's reform seems just like a desperate attempt to reduce the increasing number of failing students

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Many alternations have been made in Swedish education during the last few years

title: Swedish education in danger
text: The change that probably has been most controversial and most troublesome is the alternation of the Swedish grade system at the senior level of the nine-year compulsory school and at upper secondary school

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Earlier pupils could receive grades from one through five, where five was the highest and one the lowest

title: Swedish education in danger
text: In 1994 the grade scale was changed to grades failed, passed, passed with distinction, and passed with much distinction

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Before the change the requirements for qualifying for upper secondary school was based only on the average grade, but then when the grading system changed it was also required to pass the subjects; Swedish, Mathematics, and English

title: Swedish education in danger
text: The consequence was that many pupils failed one of the required subjects and could not move on to upper secondary

title: Swedish education in danger
text: The situation has unfortunately grown worse and politicians are desperately looking for solutions

title: Swedish education in danger
text: If believing great changes in education will solve the problem of failing pupils, this reform of teachers' education proposed by LUK could definitely be a solution to the problem

title: Swedish education in danger
text: However, I do not believe that reducing subject studies will solve any problem, only create more

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Those responsible for the reform do obviously not realise the importance of subject knowledge

title: Swedish education in danger
text: According to Helldén the expert pedagogues that have influenced LUK are more interested in educating student teachers how knowledge is formed and organised rather than letting them acquire knowledge

title: Swedish education in danger
text: This is presumably an attempt to add more pedagogic to teachers' education programs, which certainly is of great importance

title: Swedish education in danger
text: However, I believe that the present teachers' education programs include enough of pedagogic

title: Swedish education in danger
text: There is definitely no need for such a pedagogic form earlier mentioned, which seems more like mumbo jumbo than a serious suggestion

title: Swedish education in danger
text: In school children learn basic knowledge for future studies at college and University

title: Swedish education in danger
text: This basic knowledge is based on simple facts and uncomplicated structure, and does not require knowledge of how knowledge is formed and organised when teaching it

title: Swedish education in danger
text: What is considerable, however, is that teachers know their subjects

title: Swedish education in danger
text: If reducing subject studies to less than half of what the present teacher's education programs have, teachers might lose their authority as being an academically learned teacher

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Pupils or their parents may question teachers' ability to teach if they do not have enough subject knowledge

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Having authority is an essential part of being a teacher

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Teachers must feel their pupils respect them and that they can share their knowledge and play an important role in their pupils' learning

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Teachers of today has a base of subject knowledge, they know their subjects and feel secure about teaching

title: Swedish education in danger
text: However, the future teacher role seems very worrying if LUK's reform is approved in the Swedish parliament

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Teacher will not longer be teaching their pupils, but guiding them through the world of knowledge as experts in how knowledge is formed and organised

title: Swedish education in danger
text: Some politicians might believe LUK's reform of teachers' education programs will solve the problem of failing pupils and other problems they earlier caused

title: Swedish education in danger
text: However, hopefully most politicians will realise the absurdity of this suggestion

title: Swedish education in danger
Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: The question if homosexuals should be allowed to adopt has been debated from time to time and has recently been under discussion on a conference held by the Social Democrats

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: This has brought the matter to our attention again and many voices for and against homosexuals' right to adopt have of late made themselves heard

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: No matter concerning children is to be taken lightly, especially not matters concerning children who are in a vulnerable position, as adopted children often are

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: However, I think that more often than not, when people say that they are against homosexuals adopting, they really let their prejudice speak rather than their concern whether something is beneficial or not for a child

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: In the above mentioned article Susanne Eberstein says that she is afraid that some people are more interested in homosexual rights than children's welfare owing to the fact that they want to be politically correct and because of this the children will suffer

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: I do not think that people in general are so biased that they would make a decision unfavourable for children, even if they want to be politically correct

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: No matter how important gay rights are, it is not likely that homosexuals and those working to further their cause would stop at nothing and use children as instruments in their strife for equality

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Eberstein further claims that we should not allow homosexuals to adopt since we do not know how children would be affected from growing up in a family with parents of the same sex

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: The question is then how we are supposed to find out if we have no empirical data

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: What we do know is that no child benefits from starvation, living in the streets or in a loveless orphanage

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Is it not then desirable that as many people as possible should be willing to adopt, regardless of whether they are gay or straight

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Some people fear that the children would take after their parent's example and become homosexual too, but that is a puerile argument and has little to do with reality

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: If that were the reason why some people are homosexuals, then there would not be many homosexuals in the world, since most children live with heterosexual parents

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: An adopted child has to meet with many difficulties, which of course should not be ignored

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Having to face a new country with different culture and customs, and people with another colour of the skin who speak a language other than one's own, can undoubtedly be strenuous for anyone

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: However, most children are adopted when they are babies and while they might still look different from their friends, their biggest problem, when they get older, is probably to face the fact that they have adoptive parents

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Eberstein argues that since it is such a trauma for a child to be adopted we should not add to their suffering by letting parents of the same sex adopt it, seeing that the attitude prevalent in society towards homosexuals is sometimes rather negative

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: I strongly believe that this means that we have to change the current mentality in society rather than perpetuating it by ruling out homosexuals as adoptive parents

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: The final argument Eberstein has got is that children do not want to be seen as 'different' or 'strange', and she suggests that they could be bullied in school for having two mothers or two fathers

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: However, this would require that children deem homosexual parents to be something abnormal or even appalling, and as far as I know no child is born with prejudices

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Again the problem stems from attitudes found in society, this time manifested in the ignorant views of narrow-minded parents who pass on their opinions to their offspring

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Furthermore, it is not necessarily so that children will be harassed in school for having parents of the same sex

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: The teacher Eva Lindvall writes in an article in Aftonbladet that she has not experienced that harassment in school is about an individual victim's family situation or background but rather group dynamics and intolerance

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: She, for example, has got a boy in her class with two fathers who has never been bullied or teased

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Lindvall therefore concludes that children with homosexual parents do not run a greater risk of being harassed than other children

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Consequently, I think that the root of the matter is the negative attitudes that homosexuals sometimes encounter in society

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: The solution is not to prevent homosexuals from adopting but to change these negative attitudes

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: An important step towards a more open-minded society would then be allowing homosexual couples to adopt

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: In her article "Barnens bästa går före homorättigheter" in Aftonbladet, Susanne Eberstein (s), member of the Swedish Riksdag, argues that homosexuals should not be allowed to adopt children

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: She states that children must not be used as instruments in the struggle against prejudice and that we must postpone a decision allowing homosexuals to adopt if it is doubtful that it is beneficial for the adopted child

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Eberstein claims that the adoption is often a traumatic experience in itself and to then face the negative attitudes towards homosexuals sometimes found in society might prove too much

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Children, she states, do not want to be seen as different, to deviate from the norm as they might if they have two parents of the same sex

title: Adoption Rights for Homosexuals
text: Sources: Aftonbladet "Barnens bästa går för homorättigheter" 2001-11-03 "Barn föds inte med fördomar" 2001-11-07
