title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: In July 1 1999 the Swedish law against the buying of sexual services became legal

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: This means that a person who buys sex from a prostitute can be prosecuted and convicted as a criminal but prostitution, the selling of sex for money, is still not a crime

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: It is this law Sven Sundman calls illogical, unlawful and even perverse in his correspondent in Svenska Dagbladet

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: I can agree with Sundman in a way

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: When prostitution is discussed in connection with the previously mentioned law there is no doubt that the selling of the service is as criminal as the buying of it

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: It is pure logic

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: This is Sundman's main point and I don't argue against that

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: What I want to discuss is some of his arguments and also some of his choices of words

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: "In the Middle Ages there were no demand for cars, TV, computers or Internet," says Sundman meaning that if prostitution would be prohibited and declared illegal the demand for sexual services would decrease considerably since the supply of these services would be reduced

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: That the supply affects the demand is a well- known fact but prostitution is not a convenient technical invention like the car or the TV

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: The sexual need is not a demand created by businessmen and smart advertising or a need developed through the human's constant strives to make life easier

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Prostitution is about people and as long as there are people there will be a need for these kinds of services no matter what the legislation says

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Prostitution is said to be "the oldest profession in the world" and to be talking about "supply" and "demand" in this issue is quite vain

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: To generalize like Sundman does when he dismisses the people who buy sex as "perverts" is a strange and ignorant move from someone as highly educated as the Bachelor of Laws Sven Sundman

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: He has got some quite strong arguments to prove his points then why does he have to spice his correspondent with his own prejudiced opinions? These "perverts" can be all kinds of people

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Lonely people with contact-disabilities, people in need of sex plain and simple or of course they can have all kinds of perversions

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Not all of them are husbands and fathers shattering families

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: It may seem as if I am defending these people but I argue that this is a serious matter that has to be looked upon with objectivity

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: These people are individuals with individual reasons for their actions and not a generic group of people to dismiss as "perverts"

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: I claim that this statement affects Sundman's credibility

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Not that he doesn't know what he is talking about, I am sure he knows all there is to know about legal matters and legislation

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: To challenge his knowledge in these matters would be rather stupid of me who knows practically nothing about it

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: But to generalize in this way when a serious matter like prostitution is discussed is unfair

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Things are never only black or white, most of the time they are different shades of grey

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: One problem in this issue is women/men who are being forced into prostitution in some way

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Sundman claims that if prostitution was declared illegal that problem could be solved in the legislation

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: If the enforcement could be proved he/she would have "no criminal intention to the crime"

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: This would make the legislation "lawful and trustworthy"

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: It sounds good and fair

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: I agree with this and I can follow his thought but a few questions pops up in my mind

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: How can enforcement be proved? What if it can't be proved? He has noted this himself and uses as example politicians who goes to a prostitute and then claims not to have known that they did

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: He says that those cases "hopefully" can be handled despite the legislation

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: But he doesn't say how

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Sven Sundman is right when he says that either both/all of the constituents in the transaction should be considered as criminals or none of them

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: There should be no difference between buying and selling

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: But Sundman seems to think that everything can be solved through legislation

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: A problem in a society will not disappear just because there is a law against it

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: It must be dealt with on all levels

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: The way he reveals his opinions about people who buy sex from a prostitute is not necessary in this context

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: He is writing about a legal matter and if he wanted to draw attention to his points or add some colour to his writing he should have chosen another way to do that

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: This had the opposite effect

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: His arguments are quite strong but he could have conveyed them differently

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: In Svenska Dagbladet on Wednesday the 21 of February Sven Sundman, Bachelor of Laws, writes about the Swedish law against the buying of sexual services

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: He asserts that this law is a failure

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Sundman argues that there is no logic in this law

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: If buying sex is a crime then prostitution should be considered as complicity in the crime

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Either both should be punished or none of them

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
text: Sundman argues for criminalization of prostitution and claims that the demand for prostitution would decrease, that it is the only way to protect the women and men involved in prostitution and that if the legislation would be changed so that if enforcement could be proved there would be no charges

title: Sundman should focus on his legal points
THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Minority languages are often subordinated to major languages and convey the fear that their future is quite limited, uncertain

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: After reading Nicole Ren s response to Txema G

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Crespo s inquiries about the present situation of minority languages in the world, I personally disagree with her

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: For I believe that the survival of minority languages, such as Basque and French (in Canada), does not depend on the Internet, but on other factors that I will trace through out this essay

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: To be more specific, I will concentrate on the situation of the Basque language

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Before doing anything else, I find it necessary to briefly introduce and explain the situation of the Basque language, so that a better idea of what I am going to deal with in the following paragraphs is attained

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: The Basque language is a very old language (more than 3000 years) of unknown origin and only spoken in a very restricted region of northern Spain, the Basque Country

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: The language is the pillar of the Basque Culture and Tradition

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: In this respect, it would be a great pity for all the Basques (I include myself) to see our own mean of communication restricted to the Internet

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: If we want the Basque language not to become a mere linguistic product of the media that is gaining control of everything within its reach, we have to consider two determining factors

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: In the first place, I believe that the future of the Basque language is in the hands of the Basque people

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: The native speakers are the ones who can speak it and should compel others to learn it

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: According to a Basque idiom, a language does not disappear because those who do not know it do not learn it, but because those who know it do not speak it: Hizkuntza bat ez da galtzen ez dakitenek ikasten ez dutelako, dakitenek hitzegiten ez dutelako baizik

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: In this way, we could say that sociostructural factors, the fact that one s colleagues or mates are Basque, as well as psycholinguistic factors, one s ability and determination to speak Basque, have a deep influence in the usage and survival of the language

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: If people who have no knowledge of Basque surround a native speaker, then his/her use of it will be affected

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Nevertheless, it has been proved that we, the Basque, are very loyal to our language and that through many generations and centuries we have done our best to preserve it and reinforce it ( ARGIA Aldizkaria, 2001)

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Secondly, another crucial factor for the survival of the Basque language without the necessity of being confined to the net is the normalization of Basque in all the fields: administration, education, law, etc

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: As a matter of fact, ten or fifteen years ago the presence of Basque in school, university, the media, and administration was not so well known as it is today ARGIA Aldizkaria, 2001)

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Nowadays, as N

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Ren states in one of her responses to Txema G

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Crespo, many companies use Basque in their management ( EL PA S, 2001)

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Besides, in the field of education more and more schools and universities are offering courses and degrees in Basque, with the purpose of providing people with the opportunity to pursue their studies in Basque

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: In that case, they avoid the loss of the language, strengthen its usage, and secure its existence

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: In short, if these factors above explained succeed, then, there will be no use in worrying about the Basque language being limited to the Internet

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: For the people will continue speaking Basque among them and will go on teaching it to non-native speakers and to other generations

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: And, who knows, there may come the day when Basque comes out of the label of minority languages

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: A great achievement and gratification would that suppose, taking into account all the work that has been done, for all the Basques who believe that their language is one of the most important notions of their identity

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: The Future of minority Languages is on the Internet ( EL PAS , Feb

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: 28th 2001) Nicole Ren, manager of the French Agency in Quebec, asserts that the future of minority languages, such as the French language in Canada or Basque in Spain, is on the Internet

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: She argues that these languages are usually submitted to major languages: French has many difficulties to be introduced into the business world, due to the power of English in Canada

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: The Basque language, however, is being used in the management of some companies, which receive a subvention from the Basque Government for recovering Basque in the working world

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
text: Nicole Ren finally states that the Government of Quebec has supported the presence of French on the net, and believes that the survival of minority languages lies on such media

title: THE FUTURE OF MINORITY LANGUAGES IS NOT ON THE INTERNET!
Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Sweden is a monarchy but the monarch has no political power

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: His tasks are solely ceremonious and representative

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Despite the fact that the king should remain neutral in his official statements, the king as well as the queen have made statements lately that either could be classed as political or seen as personal opinions

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: For example has the queen expressed a personal opinion about the conflict in the Middle East that may have affected the reputation of Sweden in that area

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: If the royal house continues to choose sides in the political debate, then the debate will be affected

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: To prevent this from happening, Morgan Johansson suggests that the standing committee on the constitution of the Riksdag should be able to examine how the statesman manages his tasks

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Johansson argues in his article that the power committee should therefore function as the will of the people

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Johansson has further arguments to support his view and he also gives a brief history of our democracy

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: I will begin with a short description of this historical background and then present Johansson's arguments as well as my counter-arguments

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Until the breakthrough of democracy in Sweden in 1918-1921, the political power had been divided between the king and his government on one side and the Riksdag on the other

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: The Riksdag consisted of men with properties and therefore a reform was needed on all levels

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: The breakthrough of democracy consisted of two reforms: one of the Riksdag and one of the general division of power within the political system

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Parlamentarism (when the government is overlooked by the Riksdag instead of by the king) turned out to be a democratic alternative

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Despite the reforms, until 1974 the form of government that prevailed was one that saw the king as the leader of the country

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Therefore an agreement was made in 1971 between the political parties that restricted the political power of the king in favour of ceremonial and representative tasks

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: But since then there has been no follow-up of how the head of State manages his tasks and if he remains within his domain

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: This is looked upon as a major flaw within the Swedish political system by Johansson

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: His arguments for the formation of a scrutinizing committee are based on statements made by the king and the queen in the media recently

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: When the king has opinions about the mad-cow disease they may seem harmless but still they are his personal opinions

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Johansson argues that, especially in the media climate of today, statements of this kind make it possible for officials to effect the general debate as well as exercise power

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: It is questionable if the monarch possibly can remain a credible representative for the country while choosing sides in controversial debates, Johansson asserts

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: By doing this, Johansson wants to remind the reader of the fact that Sweden is a democracy where the public has the power to express their meaning by voting in general elections

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: No single person should have more power then anybody else in a parliamentary form of government

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: But Johansson forgets that in a democracy, the individuals have the same right to express their own opinion, irrespective of what social background they have

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Any restrictions of this freedom of speech could affect the opinions of a society that already has questioned the role of the royal house

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: With a scrutinizing committee, the royal famlily may become even more anonymous and remote than it is today and the king would probably lose much of his general support

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: One could say that these personal statements that have been made are symbols of general trends in society

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: For example have political leaders made similar personal statements in the media that have been criticized

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Johansson exempliflies in his article the negative effects of personal statements by mentioning an opinion expressed by the queen that had negative effects for the peace-process in the Middle East

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: There is no doubt that the opinion expressed was made by an uninformed person and it was by no means eligible

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Yet one must see it as a statement made by a person with neither enough knowledge nor direct political power to affect the politics of the current area in any specific direction

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: If this individual is deprived of her freedom of speech, then who will be the next one? The fact that there is a current debate in the media concerning the role of political or royal leaders is positive

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: But a debate presupposes a freedom of speech that is independent of higher instance

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Any restriction of this freedom acts as a signal to the rest of society; a society that consists of individuals with different opinions as well as backgrounds

title: Let the freedom of speech include all
text: Source: Morgan Johansson, "Låt KU granska kungahuset"DN Debatt (3/3 2001)

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Resent studies has shown that drug usage is increasing and that narcotics exist in closer contact with younger students

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: One of the studies I am referring to, is carried out by Centralförbundet för Alkohol- och Narkotikaupplysning, CAN

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: According to the CAN survey, 1 out of 10 students attending the 9th grade of the senior level has on one or several occasions used drugs

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Compared with statistic surveys from 1995 this is an increase by 50%

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The results are in line with other inquiries; for example, a study made by the social welfare service which is a 5 year long investigation series on students', in the second grade, upper secondary school, drug habits

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: According to the latter, 1 out of 5 pupils has tried narcotics

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Of the ones that have used drugs, 40% were under the age of 16 and 11% under the age of 13 (1995: 3%) the first time they tried narcotics

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Both studies show that the debut age for drug use has gone down and that the usage is increasing

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: They also demonstrate that the pupils' attitudes towards drugs and drug policies have become more liberal than before

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Why then, do more and more young students take drugs? One answer to this question could be the students more liberal and tolerant attitude toward drugs the youths ignore and disclaim the dangers with drugs (according to the police authorities)

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: In an article about drug abuse, one could read about a seventeen-year-old boy using ecstasy almost every weekend

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: He described the information received in school, concerning narcotics, to be very one-sided and that he felt he couldn't relate to it

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: I think he is making an important point

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The information you receive in school often describe worn-out drug addicts sleeping in street corners, and imply that that is where you will end up if you take drugs

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Although it is not wholly unlikely that something like this could happen to you, if using drugs, it is very difficult for young pupils to identify themselves with this picture

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: There is a big difference between this and taking a few pills at private parties on weekends, many of these young users are often well behaved and very social, which makes the gap and differences between them and freaked-out addicts seem huge

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Another cause to the increasing number of younger students using drugs could be due to high group pressure and a desire to fit in

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Youths are very vulnerable and easily affected when they are in their early teens

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Most of them are trying to figure out who they are, how to act and are often deadly afraid of being classified as outsiders

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: It is commonly known that most people who start smoking do it because they want to fit in, to belong to a group or just want to be accepted, the same goes for drinking alcohol

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Taking drugs is the third step some of us take in order to be accepted

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Drugs have become the new rage and are used as a supplement to alcohol and smoking

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: You may think that narcotics are a considerable more dangerous mean of intoxication and that people can and do say no, but keep in mind that most youths get in contact with narcotics when they are very young and at such early ages, it is not easy to say no

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The desire to fit in makes you do almost anything

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Also, when living such in close contact with drugs where they are easy to obtain, which school pupils do nowadays, they are running a high risk of trying it themselves

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The more that start using drugs, the more will follow

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Some other factors contributing to this increase are, to begin with, the fact that it isn't very likely that you will be caught by the police

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Most youths take drugs almost exclusively at private parties and usually know the person selling them their narcotics, from before

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: Another factor is that Sweden has not had that many problems with drugs in the past, it has not been one of the bigger social issues and I believe that is why this problem has been able to expand more freely

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The number of ecstasy pills confiscated so far this year goes up to 150 000, compared with last years 75 000 pills

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: This trend will keep on getting bigger and bigger if we are unable to work out some sort of solution or way to stop it in the nearer future

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES
text: The expansion of narcotics in Sweden is being made very fast, which makes this is a problem that needs to be paid attention to and to be acknowledged by society

title: THE INCREASING NUMBER OF YOUTHS USING DRUGS AT LOWER AGES

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: In an article published in Dagens Nyheter (Jan

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: 30, 2001) paediatrician Hugo Lagercrantz argues that not all prematurely born children should be saved

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: His arguments require a response and the stand- point of this essay is that all prematurely born children should be saved

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: It needs to be pointed out that this is a complex issue which cannot be dealt with fully on a few pages

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: This essay is a response to the arguments Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz has put forward in his article

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Also note that the discussion only concerns Swedish conditions and that the chil-dren the discussion refers to are foremost those born between week 22 and 25

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: To get the right perspective it is important to know that in Sweden only a hundred children a year are born between week 22 and 25

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The first of Lagercrantz's two arguments to why not all prematurely born children should be saved and also one of the most common counter arguments is the risk of handicaps

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: There is a risk of neurological handicaps for prematurely born children but it is difficult to determine how great this risk is as follow-up studies are being made presently

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Four physicians from Umeå and Uppsala claim in another article that there is 80 per cent chance of survival, without any severe handicaps as a conse-quence, for children born in week 25 (Dagens Nyheter, Feb

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz's comment on handicaps is the following

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: "To be born prematurely and be saved by 'courageous doctors' is unfortunate not only for the surviving child if he or she becomes severely handicapped but also for the whole family

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: One of the parents will most likely have to sacrifice the rest of his or her life for this child

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Brothers and sisters will be second priority

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: " Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz states in a few sentences that a handicapped person cannot live a happy and fulfilling life and that he or she will be a burden to the other members of the family

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz says that he is a representative for the children

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: the child's right to be born to a good life", he writes

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: One might then ask oneself if he is the one who sets up the standards for what a good life is? There need to be a change in society's view of handicapped people

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: They must be looked upon as resources not burdens

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz's second argument is that the lack of resources at Swedish hospitals makes it impossible to save all prematurely born children

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: He writes: "We [in the County of Stockholm] have 32 incubators

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: and there are around 10 000 children born a year

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: " He compares these numbers to the number of incubators in Lille in France which is 60 and the same number of births

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: He also criticises the low wages for nurses working with children

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Who would not agree with Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz on these points? It is a great problem that there are not enough resources at Swedish hospitals

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: There are no easy solutions to this problem but in the article, referred to earlier in this essay, an interesting view is put forward

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The four physi-cians compare the numbers of prematurely born children who survive in Uppsala and Umeå to the corresponding numbers for other university hospitals

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: In Uppsala/Umeå 53 per cent of the children born in week 23 and 24 survive

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The corresponding number for the other university hospitals is 44 per cent

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Their conclusion is that the chance of survival depends on the physi-cian's attitude towards his or her task

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: A negative attitude will affect the whole team and their work

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The problem with a lack of resources cannot be ignored but a changed attitude towards the task of saving prematurely born children is where we need to begin

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: What Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz did not discuss at all in his article but which has to be mentioned is the patient's worth

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Can we allow any external circumstances to affect what medical care a patient receives? A prematurely born child should be judged according to the same principles as all other patients; all humans should have the same worth

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: As a response to Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz's article this essay has dealt with the issue of why all prematurely born children should be saved

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: As stated early on in this essay this is a complex issue

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The risk of handicaps exists but is not as great as one might think

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: More important to remember is that it is not such a "disaster" to be handicapped as Dr

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: Lagercrantz wants to depict it

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: The lack of resources is a problem but just as no other patient group should have to suffer because of this neither should prematurely born children

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: That must be the attitude; more resources not less medical care

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
text: What it comes down to is how we value life- pall humans should have the same worth

title: Why All Prematurely Born Children Should Be Saved
Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: Lars Holger Holm's article in "Finanstidningen" is surely aimed to raise a debate and agitate or shock the average reader

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: Unfortunately, it is clumsily written and it is difficult to follow, due to its illogical and incoherent argumentation

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: Since the content is so controversial, Holm should have put some more effort in making his stance clear

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: As it is now, it is impossible to interpret the content in any other way than as a defence for pederasts, on the condition that they are talented enough

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: I also object to the irrelevant use of pregnant words and hyperboles meant to stealthily induce the feelings he wants the reader to feel

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: That is an obvious sign of his lack of sufficient arguments for his cause

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?
text: And what is the cause? The heading "The outcasts are silenced" is followed by the introductory text "Imagine if it was possible to eradicate all perversions of the human race

title: Would anybody agree to be slaughtered in the name of art?

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Lately there have been some debating about a proposal of imposing tuition fees for students at universities and collages in Sweden

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: You could read about this in Uppsalas Nya Tidning in February 13 written by Johnny Andersson

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: "It's time for University fees" is his firm title, which I'm sure many students find outrageous

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Therefore it is my intention to argue against this article and reveal its weak points

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: But before I do that I'm going to summarize the article and its main arguments

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Johnny Andersson is of the opinion that mere state financing of the universities in Sweden is not enough to keep a high quality as required

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: He states that the ambition to have 50 % of all upper secondary school students proceeding with the higher education is too resource exacting, so something should be done about that

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Why not follow the example of England, USA, Australia etc and let students pay for their education

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Andersson argues that the money is necessary for research operations at the universities, which is essential for innovation in society

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: He also asserts that if students have to pay for their education they will be more distinct in their demands for the quality of their education

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: This would, according to Andersson, result in a much better standard and quality in the higher education and raise the status of the different universities

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: In the article he wonders if tuition fees wouldn't worsen the recruitment of underrepresented groups in society

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: But his answer to that is that these groups are not applying for higher education anyway, despite the fee-free system

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: If we retain this system where only the state is providing for the universities in the country, it (the state) will be forced to dilute the resources until the quality will get low and universities will be driven out of business by other national or international education bodies

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: If Johnny Andersson's proposal would be implemented, it would only have negative effects on the Swedish higher education

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: The first thing to happen would be decreased recruitment to universities, which would be disasterous for the future

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: The main goal is to have as many high educated citizens as possible, but if students would be forced to pay fees in order to obtain their education, those with inferior economic situations would become discouraged and deterred, and choose to work instead of study

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: The government's policy should be to avoid a rising ignorant working class and provide free education even for the underprivileged

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Today more than half of all students come from homes lacking academic education, but still it is more probable that a child from a better-off family continues with higher education than a child from a working-class home

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: If tuition fees become reality it would get even worse, and fat pockets would get precedence over aptitude and ability, when entering an university

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: In other words, increased class distinctions would become a fact

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Another thing that tuition fees would imply is that students would be much more dependent upon their parents when it comes to choice of education

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
text: Once again, it would be up to the economic situation of the parents to decide where their children be educated, or if they'd be educated at all

title: Objection to tuition fees in higher education
To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: In Henrik Sunné's article (UNT February 13 2001), he argues that we should legalise drugs to get rid of the Mafia in Sweden

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: He asserts that drugs are the Mafia's biggest source of income and that if we got rid of this source we would prevent the Mafia from spreading

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: He also argues that there would be less prostitution, fewer murders, rapes, robberies and drug addicts

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Furthermore he asserts that no Swedish addict would pay hundreds of SKR for a specific amount of a drug if he/she could get the same amount a lot cheaper in a store

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This is surely a very naive point of view and in this essay I will argue against his arguments and the legalisation of drugs in general

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Henrik Sunné's first argument is that if we legalised drugs we would weaken the Mafia and prevent them from spreading, because drugs are their foremost source of income and the hub in their criminal business

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: His intention, to get rid of the Mafia, is good but it's a very stupid way to deal with this problem, it would bring a lot of other threats to our society

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: The Mafia would find something else to deal with to get the money they need to run their business

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Examples of income sources might be illegal dealings with weapons, liquor, tobacco and smuggling of people

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: One good example of this is the Mafia that existed in USA in the 30s

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Their biggest income came from smuggling liquor and this is proof that the Mafia will find other ways to get the money roll in

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: So to say that Sunné's idea, legalising drugs, would reduce the Mafia's income is not right

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: According to Sunné, legalising drugs would also reduce the number of murders, rapes, robberies and drug addicts

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This is the second argument he uses and also one that is completely wrong

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Not fewer, but more, people would use drugs, because the drugs would be easy and legal to buy, and therefore there would be an increasing number of addicts

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Sunné tries to connect today's rapes, robberies and prostitution with those who are addicts and that it is those who commit these crimes to get money they can buy drugs for

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This might partially be true but there will probably be an increase in these criminal areas if the drugs were to become legal

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This is shown in studies made in the Netherlands where these kinds of crimes have increased due to the fact that drugs are legal there

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: And these crimes would increase because of the way drugs effect people psychologically and not because they commit them because they need the money

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Most addicts are often broke, isolated from the surrounding and those who are worst off have no contact with family or friends, which probably is a result of the way the drugs affect humans psychologically

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: So it isn't just the addicts who have to suffer but also the people in their surrounding

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: And these aren't things that go away just because you take a brake from drugs or quit using them completely

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This state of living would become more usual among our population if we were to legalise drugs

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: As we see in Sunné's last argument no Swedish addict would pay hundreds of SKR for a dose he/she can get in a store much cheaper

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: To make drugs available in stores would give a message to teenagers who probably would see it as if there is nothing wrong with doing drugs and therefore they might try it and that's an easy way to become an addict

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: There would be an increase in the number of teenagers who use drugs because even if they would put an age limit on buying drugs older people would always by for them

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Today there are grown-ups who buy liquor for teenagers who aren't allowed to buy it themselves

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This increase of young addicts would lead to more crimes, and society's cost for rehabilitation of the addicts would increase because more people would need rehabilitation

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: The cost for one addict is about 2000 SKR per night

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: History also has a proof of that legalising drugs leads to more addicts and that it is a thing that no one can win on

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: In the middle of the 60s there was an experiment where we in Sweden legalised drugs

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: This experiment left catastrophic consequences with an enormous increase of heavy addicts and a lot more deaths as result

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: So this experiment didn't succeed and two years later drugs became illegal again

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: (Margareta Nyström ordf

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: FMN-Uppsala) In short no one will gain on legalising drugs

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: There will be more crimes and more addicts and it will be more expensive for our society

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: What we must do is to help the addicts and stop the dealers

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: To legalise something that is proven to be bad for your health and might even kill you is an act of lunacy

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: So instead of giving in for those who want the drugs to be legal we have to continue our fight against these menaces to society

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Article Summary In UNT February 13 2001 Henrik Unné argues strongly for legalisation of drugs in Sweden

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: By doing this he thinks that we will get rid of the Mafia

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: He mentions that the Mafia kills innocent people, turn women into sex slaves and blackmail honest businessmen and that the Mafia's biggest income comes from their sale of drugs

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: So legalising drugs would prevent the Mafia from expanding

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: By legalising drugs, the Mafia's greatest income would disappear and it would threaten their existence

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
text: Furthermore he argues that there would be fewer murders, rapes, robberies and addicts and that no Swedish addict would pay hundreds of SKR in the street for the same amount of drugs that can be bought for only a couple of SKR in a store

title: To Legalise Drugs is an Act of Lunacy
Let the Royal family speak
text: Morgan Johansson argues, in an article from Upsala Nya Tidning March 3rd 2001, that the King, and all the members of the Royal family, should be more or less controlled in what the say to avoid awkward and difficult situations for the Swedish politicians

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: He bases this on the agreement in Torekov 1971, where it was decided that Sweden were to remain a Monarchy but the King, or Queen, were to have no political power and only serve as ceremonial persons

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: A good agreement, according to Morgan Johansson, since the Swedish people may want the King, but practically no one want him to rule the country

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: I agree that the King should not have any political power, but I do not think it is right to try to limit his statements in political issues even though the Royal family is an exceptional stepping-stone for those who want to turn a debate in some direction

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: I believe that members of the Royal family should be able to say anything they want and according to the right of free speech, they can

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: Mr Johansson also uses history to give strength to his proposal

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: He tells us how parliamentary government were introduced in Sweden and how it was put to a difficult test in 1914 when King Gustav V held his courtyard speech attacking the government which ended with the resignation of Prime Minister Karl Staaff

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: I get the feeling that Morgan Johansson is afraid that something like that could happen

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: However I cannot even imagine King Carl XVI Gustaf making a speech attacking Göran Persson and if it would happen, it is not likely that Göran Persson would have to resign

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: You could safely say that times have changed and I do not think Morgan Johansson has to be afraid of the King making Göran Persson resign

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: Spurred by King Carl XVI Gustaf's statement that the debate about the mad-cow disease was exaggerated, Morgan Johansson throws out a question whether it is possible to keep the King as a trustworthy symbol for our country if it is obvious that he has chosen a side in a highly controversial matter

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: I do not see why the King's credibility as a symbol for Sweden would suffer only because he lets the Swedish people know what he thinks in a discussed topic

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: Should not he, the King of Sweden, be allowed to tell what he believes? Morgan Johansson also reflects over the year 2000 where there were almost only good things said about Sweden in other countries

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: You could read about the Holocaust conference and the opening of the bridge over Öresund

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: One of the negative news was, according to Johansson, created by Queen Silvia when she, in an interview last October, said that she could not understand why Palestinians sent their children out in the streets to throw rocks towards the Israeli police

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: Johansson says that the Queen's view of the conflict in the Middle East is completely off target and I do not know enough about the conflict to argue with him, but he also says that it was evident that the Queen had picked a side

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: For me it is not that clear, but what I know of the Queen's engagements is that she is always looking after the children in every situation

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: If she did take the children's side in that discussion I would not object at all, even though it got big attention in the Arab world

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: It was, however, unfortunate that there were peace talks between Israelis and Palestinians at that time and that Sweden was leading the discussions

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: I still believe in the right of free speech, even for the Royal family, why should they be an exception? I can very well agree that both the King and Queen may not always make very clever statements, but it is their right to make statements, clever or not, as any other person

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: If they were not allowed to say anything they wanted it would probably limit their engagements in different areas

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: Imagine the following scenario; a reporter comes up to the King and asks what he thinks of the current unemployment rate in Sweden

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: The King, instructed to keep neutral in political questions, answers "No comment" or "I do not know" when he instead would like to have said something like "I think that the government has to do something to lower the unemployment rate"

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: A lot of strange situations could arise if the Royal family was not allowed to comment on political questions

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: All I can say to Morgan Johansson is: Let the Royal family speak

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: In an article from Upsala Nya Tidning March 3rd 2001 Morgan Johansson, a social democratic member of the Riksdag, argues that the standing committee of the constitution should have power to examine how the head of State is handling his duties

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: He demonstrates this by showing different examples of members of the Royal family making, according to Mr Johansson, inappropriate comments in the political world

title: Let the Royal family speak
text: He also backs up his arguments by describing the long and slow process, in which the King's role was reduced to a ceremonial and representative one, towards democracy in Sweden and implies that we cannot let the Royal family gain any sort of political power

title: Let the Royal family speak
Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: When I was younger I was against the proposal that homosexuals should be allowed to adopt children

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: I was opposed to it, just as Lindén, because I was thinking about the children and the problems it will cause for them, in school and among their friends

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Maybe I have become older and wiser now, but I also believe that society has changed to a more tolerant view of homosexuals

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Now I can't see any major problems with a family constellation with two mothers or two fathers

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: They already exist, with or without difficulties, exactly as any "ordinary" family has

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: I also think that if we change the rules in our society there will be a change of what is seen as acceptable and the public norms

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: If we take steps towards a more tolerant society by allowing "gayadoptions", it will encourage people to be more open and proud of their sexuality, which is positive

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: It is not your sexual preferences that decide if you are a good parent or not

text: We should carefully examine other qualities such as ability to love and responsibility when we decide if someone is appropriate to adopt a child or not

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Secondly, I have come across another heavy counter argument saying that children who are put up for adoption are often traumatised by earlier experience, and should therefore be treated in the best possible way, which is absolutely true

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Some people argue that we should avoid further complications in adopted children's life by putting them in a family consisting of two fathers or two mothers and that children have the right to have one father and one mother when they come to Sweden

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: However, it is better to live in a homosexual family in Sweden than to live in the streets or in an orphanage

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: There are a huge number of children that would benefit from coming to Sweden

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: And it is far from every child that has a mother and a father in his/her life

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Thirdly, to answer the objection that other countries won't allow their children to be adopted to Swedish gay families: Of course we won't force people to give their babies to homosexual couples when they are against it but I don't see that as a problem

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: We can control which baby goes to which family and I am sure that some people in some countries will allow their children to be adopted by gay parents

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: In places such as Iceland and California they have already consented that they could send children to gay couples

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: And if this proposal to allow "gayadoptions" goes through, there is nothing that implies that there will be a huge demand for babies from homosexual couples

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: In the beginning there will be few "gayadoptions" so there will surely be enough babies

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: A fourth counter argument that I will deal with asserts that there is no research saying that it isn't harmful for babies to be adopted by homosexual parents, and we shouldn't use babies as guinea pigs to experiment on

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: "Gayadoptions" is a new thing and for that reason there hasn't been much research in the subject

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: We can get hold of much more information than they could when they first started adopting children from other countries, to Sweden

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: Briefly put: I used to think that the adopted children to gay parents would suffer because of their parents but now I think that the Swedish people are ready for this

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: A main reason to why I think "gayadoptions" are a good thing is the fact that there are so many children in the world that would have a better life if they were living in Sweden with homosexual parents, compared to the way they live their life now
