text: Another problem is that other countries will not send children to Sweden if we will allow homosexuals to adopt children

title: Homosexual couples should have the right to be tried as adopting parents
text: In the end he turns towards the reader to ask: what do you think?

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Summary of article "Barnens bästa kommer först" from readers debate section in UNT 3 -2-2001 The author of this article (Mikael Oscarsson, riksdagsman (kd) argues against homosexual's right to have an equal chance to adoption as heterosexuals

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: In the article he argues that "it is no human right for grown ups to have children" but children has a human right to have parents

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Some keypoints in this article are: * Every child has a right to a mother and a father; children need both a female and a male for identification and normal development

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: * Children's rights organisations and adoption organisations are against homosexual adoptions

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: * Swedish opinion research on the matter shows that 60% of the Swedish people are against homosexual adoption

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: * There is no research that can indicate what will happen to a child that is adopted by homosexuals

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Mr Oscarsson argues that it would be hard for the adopted children to have "one thing more to fight, since they already have to fight against prejudice because they are different" In this essay I will argue against Mikael Oscarssons article where he states that homosexual adoptions is wrong

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: I will take up what I see as the main arguments for homosexual adoption and clearly express this in four paragraphs with a summary at the end

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Mr Oscarssons first point is that all children have a right to a mother and a father

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: The first argument that I am going to present is the fact that children all over the world is left alone without parents for one reason or the other

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: These children have the right to be brought up in a safe environment where they are loved

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Homosexual couples are as loving as any heterosexual couples

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Sexuality should not be a matter of discussion when it comes to adoption

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Homosexuals should be equally judged before an adoption, whether they are suitable parents or not

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: The same procedure should be applied for both homosexuals and heterosexuals

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: One common argument for people that are against homosexual adoption is that homosexuals are egocentric since they want children as a right and are not thinking of the child's best

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Many people claim that children are adopted for there own good

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: No child comes to the world for "its own good", peoples desire to get children is what brings them into this world

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Usually that desire is enough for a person to become a good parent, no matter of sexuality and ethnic belonging, etc

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Mr Oscarssons argument is based on a idealised picture of the world which do not exist, that is why I feel that his argument is not convincing, it is more provoking than realistic since we all know how many children there are that are left without anybody to trust

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: A second argument that I would like to bring forward is that the author of the article tries to enhance his arguments by reference to different experts, a network of young adopted people (of which size is not mentioned) and public opinion research

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Claiming that only the majority has superiority in the interpretation of opinions is superstitious and not convincing at all

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: I claim that if this is an agument that is generally accepted there would be no possibility of development in society

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: If we had argued the same way about child flogging we would still have a society where it is generally accepted

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: In the middle of 20 Th century it was still commonly accepted to beat children for an educational purpose

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: It is obvious today that people in Sweden do not accept child flogging

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: What I mean by that is that people at large, and even experts changes their opinion after the law has been changed

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: I believe that people will change their minds about homosexuality and therefore also the arguments against homosexual adoptions

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: I believe that this debate will seem shameful and awkward after a hundred years, and can be compared with how we today look at the debate hundred years ago about women's franchise reform

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Mr Oscarsson avoids discussing the most essential question (=to be able to give love, in the full sense of the word, is not a matter of sexuality) when he makes his disition that homosexuals should not be allowed to adopt children

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Since no one today knows why an individual is homosexual, I chose to look upon this "condition" in the same way as I look upon the colour of hair or skin a person has

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: No body would come up with the idea that it would be wrong for albinos or black people to adopt children

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: The important thing is that the grown up adopter can provide a safe environment physically, spiritually and socially

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: According to UN children's rights convention article 6 "every child have the right to survival and development"

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: I certainly believe "survival and development" for child is better reached in a loving home where the child is looked upon as an individual, rather than in an orphanage, where they are not likely to have any emotional stimuli or physical touch

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: In contrast to Mr Oscarssons last point I would like to argue that children are born under different circumstances all over the world and raised in all kinds of homes with no "covering research" that indicates what will happen to them later in life

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Furthermore I am convinced that all children get from their upbringing different experience which effects their personality and behaviour

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: To grow as persons we must come to reconciliation with our own history

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: To blame all our bad behaviour on for example a sad childhood shows that this reconciliation has not been done

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: An individual chooses to not take responsibility for his/hers actions or bad behaviour

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Not until we understand that we have a choice, we can learn how to take responsible for our lives

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: This applies to all human beings adopted and non adopted

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: You can not hide from the difficulties of life, no matter how big or small they are

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Consequently I what to stress that this is not an issue about "the children's best" but rather a question about human development

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: True love is a matter of tolerance, diversity among people must be accepted

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Unfortunately people that argues against homosexual adoption only discuss the surface of a most important issue

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: The question is not whether they should be allowed to adopt or not but rather if they should be considered as equals

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: As far as I can see society does not yet consider homosexuals as equal

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Society will change their opinion about this but I believe that the law has to come first

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
text: Personally I am convinced that it is a matter of time until this problem is solved

title: Homosexual Parents is Better than a Society Full of Prejudice
Premature Children
text: The question of life has long been a hot topic

title: Premature Children
text: The recent discovery that a foetus can be saved at the same week during the pregnancy when it is still legal to abort it has evoked the debate

title: Premature Children
text: In the article from Dagens Nyheter, January 30 2001, the paediatrician Hugo Lagercrantz argues that since there is a 50 percent risk that the premature child will get retardation, physicians in consultation with the parents should not try to save all premature children

title: Premature Children
text: Are there any causes that could affect a patient's value and the physician's obligation to give sufficient medical care? Lagercrantz seems to believe so, but I do not and in this essay I will present two arguments which proves this

title: Premature Children
text: Firstly the right of life and secondly the right of all human beings to obtain medical care

title: Premature Children
text: This is a topic that involves several different aspects such as whether the law of abortion should be changed, the question of euthanasia and the question of what is a worthy life

title: Premature Children
text: However, since this is a limited essay I will restrict myself to the two arguments mentioned above

title: Premature Children
text: Firstly, I believe that all human beings possess the same value and the same right to live, regardless of age

title: Premature Children
text: I believe that a person's human value and right to live does not correlate to his or her age

title: Premature Children
text: A seventy-year-old woman does not have a greater right to live than a baby born too early has

title: Premature Children
text: The premature child should therefore be judged according to the same principles as any other person, young or old

title: Premature Children
text: Strangely enough, as Lagercrantz seems to believe, premature children apparently do not have the same right to live as fully developed children have when there is a risk of getting a disease

title: Premature Children
text: However, according to present legislation, the foetus gains full value of protection when it is viable, that is when it outside the uterus, can accept the help modern medical care can provide, with a chance to grow into a human being

title: Premature Children
text: Therefore, the physicians must try to save the child

title: Premature Children
text: Moreover, I feel that we, ordinary people as well as physicians, do not obtain the right to rule over other people's lives

title: Premature Children
text: Life is a gift and is not something that we have power over

title: Premature Children
text: Secondly, Lagercrantz writes that there is a 50 percent risk of the premature child ending up with retardation and therefore states that the physicians should not try to save severely premature children when the risk of illness is so great

title: Premature Children
text: Yes, it is inevitable that a certain number of premature children will end up with retardation, however, as the physician Fredrik Serenius et al states in an article in Dagens Nyheter from February 18 2001; there is also a risk of retardation for fully developed children who have suffered from serious illnesses after their births

title: Premature Children
text: These children would never be denied the right of medical care, nor would adults with a severe disease, and of course, this should be the case for premature children as well

title: Premature Children
text: The main task of the physicians should be to provide best possible care and this care is not reserved for any particular person, but is a right of all human beings

title: Premature Children
text: Additionally, I believe that the paediatrician's attitude is important in order to save the premature child

title: Premature Children
text: As Serenius et al states, if the physician is quite sure that the child will end up with an illness, his attitude against the rescue will probably influence the staff in the delivery room which in the end could cause the child's death

title: Premature Children
text: In conclusion, I strongly object to Lagercrantz arguments of why the physicians should not try to save all premature children

title: Premature Children
text: As stated, my belief is that we all have the same value irrespective of age and therefore we also have the right to obtain equally good medical care

title: Premature Children
text: In addition, Sweden is a well-developed country within this field and has the resources to give retarded children and their families all possible help and care

title: Premature Children
text: I believe Lagercrantz's attitude to be élitist and fear that his ideas could lead to an even more segregated society in which people can reject retarded children, children which can give us another perspective on life

title: Premature Children
Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: This argumentative essay will deal with and argument against some of Thorsten Bodekull's reasons for legalising drugs in Sweden and will take up Cannabis as a drug in particular

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Torsten Bodekull states in his article that we should follow Belgium and Holland and legalise "soft drugs" like Cannabis since it is not more dangerous than cigarettes and alcohol

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: He also claims that a person taking Cannabis will not go on to heavier drugs such as heroine

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: What he does not seem to know is that researches show that all narcotics have damaging effects on the human body-also Cannabis

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: During the sixties Cannabis was thought to be a harmless drug but all researches over the last decades show the opposite

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Cannabis is the most common drug in all parts of Sweden and is the drug that youths come in contact with and try first

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: People who smoke the drug usually feel its' effect within minutes and it may last up to eight hours depending on how high the dose is

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: The effect is delayed when eating or drinking the drug so that it lasts longer and may be more difficult to control

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: People may become both physically and psychologically dependent on Cannabis but it is the psychological addiction that is the strongest dependence and addiction can occur over a very short time period

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Some of the immediate effects of lower doses are elation, excitement and a feeling of well-being, increased pulse rate, increased appetite and increased sociability

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Common side effects are confusion, restlessness, hallucinations, anxiety or panic, concentration difficulties and impaired coordination etc

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: High doses may cause coma

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Long term effects are increased risk of lung cancer, slow confused thinking which have effects on learning, nerve damage, changes in personality, suppressed effects on sperms and an impaired immune system etc

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Even more, Cannabis used during pregnancy can cause diminished birth weight, facial deformities and heart defects

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Naturally all people who use Cannabis will not go on using heavier drugs but as the use of Cannabis increases, the tolerance level increases, and one need to take higher doses to get the same effect as in the beginning

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: This means that a person can take five to ten times higher doses than what they began with

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Therefore it is very possible that a person goes on to hard drug use as well to get an effect

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Another argument Torsten Bodekull uses in his article for legalising drugs is that regulations and laws do not stop people from using it

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: He believes that it is a waste of time and money to use resources to try to stop the occurrence of drugs since "we cannot prohibit something that already has its roots in society" He does not seem to be aware of the fact that Sweden has a low rate of drug addicts compared to many other countries and this due to our restrictive regulations

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Statistics show that if a drug is easily available among friends and in schools the consumption of it increases

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: A comparison with alcohol and narcotics show the clear connection between availability and use

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Alcohol is easy to get hold of at the liquor shop and is socially accepted in society, while narcotics are harder to get hold of and are less accepted

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: 1980 the legal use of alcohol was abused by 300 000 people whereas illegal narcotics at the same time was esteemed to be abused by between 10 000 and 14 000 people

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Societies attitude towards drugs is of great importance

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: To legalise drugs, either it is "soft drugs" such as cannabis or heavier ditto, would give the picture that there is no wrong in using it and more people would try it

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: It is confirmed by statistics that it is more common for youths to use drugs if role models like parents and relatives or friends use it

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Drugs do not belong in a democracy since it diminishes a person's ability to judge and react and therefore increases the risk of hurting innocent people

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: The abuser is prepared to go through many sacrifices to get hold of the drug; like risking job, family and friends

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: The whole welfare state is affected and need to pay for the disturbances that the abuse causes by diseases, crimes, production losses and accidents etc

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Furthermore children with parents who are drug addicts cannot help getting involved in their parent's business and are often maltreated, neglected or threatened and do of course take much damage from it

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: If we legalise drugs we also have to take the consequences and enhance the resources for maltreated children and young drug addicts

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: All drugs are habit forming which make the usage hard to control

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: People who are affected by a drug are not only a peril to themselves but also to others

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Regulations are important since statistics show that socially accepted drugs are used more frequently than illicit drugs

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: If we legalise Cannabis more young people will start using it and society will have to pay for the damages

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: It is crucial to keep our rigorous regulations also in the future

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: All facts and ideas taken from the Internet Nope's homepage about drugs where I found links to CAN and RNS that also had much facts about narcotics

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Another important source has been the Swedish police's homepage "brottsförebyggande info" and The Swedish Parliament's homepage where I read propositions written by "Folkpartiets" Lars Leijonborg

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: Some info is also taken from BBC News homepage "health"

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: The article I have chosen is a letter to the editor in "Uppsala Nya Tidning" 16 Feb 2001 and is written by Thorsten Bodekull

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: He states that the Swedish drug policy is old fashioned and that we should look at Holland and Belgium and take after their less restrictive laws and legalise soft drugs such as Cannabis

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: He does not believe that regulations lead to a society free from drugs and claims that it is a waste of resources to let politicians spend time on trying to deal with narcotic problems

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
text: According to him it is wrong to look upon drug users as criminals

title: Sweden should not legalise drugs
THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The expansion of the centre and outskirts of Uppsala is a subject widely discussed in the newspapers

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The expansion is defined in a plan called Vision Uppsala

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The aim is to build 16

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: 000 new apartments in the centre and outskirts of town before 2020

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Kollberg's editorial shows that a common opinion held among politicians is that the expansion is necessary if we want a dynamic and economically strong municipality attractive for the new business enterprises

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The fact that green areas and parks will have to be turned into high-rise blocks, parking lots and roads are of less importance

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: According to Kollberg apartments must be built where people want to live

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: I can agree that more apartments are needed and that some areas in town could be exploited for this purpose in for instance Kungsmarken and Boländerna

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: In too many cases however an extensive expansion of the inner city is in conflict with the environment and what the citizens want

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: According to Mats Åkerblom's article (UNT 28/9-00) research proves that people want to have access to green areas within a distance of 300 metres from where they live

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: This demand will be difficult to fulfil since important and valuable recreation areas and relaxing green spots risk disappearing a little at a time if nothing is done to prevent it

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Arguments about cultural and financial profit can thus be countered by pointing at lowered living conditions for the citizens

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Heavy traffic is another problem that must be dealt with effectively

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: This is already a problem as it is

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: If Uppsala is to expand public transport has to be made an efficient alternative and measures must be taken to prevent traffic from increasing

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: An argument often used in favour of the expansion and a problem that we are all aware of is that young people and students have big difficulties in finding somewhere to live

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: But the apartments that are being built are mainly not made for renting and are so expensive that young people cannot afford to buy them anyway

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: So the expansion does not favour those who would need it most

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: In his article Åkerblom suggests that politicians and high officials are those profiting most from the expansion

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Their power and privilege will increase with the growth of the municipality

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: He also takes up another interesting issue not dealt with in Kollberg's editorial

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: He asserts that a quick and extensive expansion will be expensive for the taxpayers since the investments necessary in realizing this plan will exceed current taxation

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The result is that the municipality must increase taxes

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Many negative aspects can be found against a quick expansion of the inner city

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: My intention has been to point at a few of them mainly dealing with the environmental consequences that Kollberg thinks are of minor importance to the future of those who live and wants to live in Uppsala

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: People who move to Uppsala must be aware of the fact that the city will look very different if it is exploited to the extent, which is demanded

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: I am like most people prone to act out of self-interest and that is why I want to take a stand against the new vision of Uppsala

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: I want it to be possible now as well as in the future to enjoy the green spots and the beautiful recreation areas available in Uppsala

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: I believe they are vitally important for our health and well being

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: In her editorial "En kommun i världsklass" Kerstin Kollberg claims that it is necessary for politicians to pass resolutions more quickly concerning an extensive expansion of the inner city of Uppsala

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Uppsala is an attractive municipality for new business enterprises but Kollberg fears that the lack of new apartments in attractive areas will make business owners decide to establish themselves somewhere else instead

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: This would be a great economical loss for the municipality

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: She also remarks that students who want to study in Uppsala are often forced to choose other universities in other parts of the country due to the housing shortage

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: The failure of meeting these demands quickly enough is an impediment for the growth and development of Uppsala and that of Sweden in general according to Kollberg

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: She mentions that the expansion entails that some of the now existing recreation areas must be turned into residential areas

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: Many citizens in Uppsala are against this, which is one reason why the city does not expand as quickly as Kollberg and many politicians think it should

title: THE EXPANSION OF UPPSALA
text: REFERENCES: Articles from Uppsala Nya Tidning: "En kommun i världsklass?" Kerstin Kollberg "För vem ska Uppsala växa?" Mats Åkerblom, 28/9-00

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I have read a news item in Svenska Dagbladet October 7, 2001 and I react to some of the statements concerning foetus diagnostic and test tube fertilization

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: For this reason, I will argue against these treatments because they are on one hand on the verge of being unethical and on the other too trying for those involved

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I can understand that these treatments are very important to many people but I do not agree considering the uncertainty when they select the embryos and the tamper with the woman's hormones and body

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I cannot understand either why couples expose themselves for these trials in their aspiration of having a baby of their own

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: My first argument is the ethical aspect of using the DNA technology

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I react to the father's statement in the article that the selection of embryos is more acceptable than having an abortion

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: Perhaps that method seems easier to carry out but for me is that kind of diagnostic much more frightening

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: It is too easy to do other selections than the damaged chromosomes

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: In the article the result of the couple's pregnancy was twins, one of each sex

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: Was it by chance or were they selected? There will always be questions like that when this diagnostic is used because of the possibilities of choosing embryos with special qualities

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: If the parents have the natural ability themselves, they can ask for a girl who will be blue-eyed and blonde or a tall boy

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: This is a distasteful kind of foetus diagnostic since we screen out people that we think do not fit in

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: In other words there will be no mentally retarded people or person with weaknesses in our society

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: If we accept this use of technology we show them that they are useless and also something we do not want to have around

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: The message will be that they are not human beings just a damage

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: To me it sounds awful whereas I have a cousin who has one chromosome too much (Down syndrome) and I cannot think of him as something that could have been discarded

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: My second argument is the test tube fertilization, which is the treatment that must be done before the foetus diagnostic I wrote about above

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I said the treatment seemed easier to carry out but the fact is that it takes a lot of effort from the woman and the couple's relationship

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: In the article they described that a number of eggs were picked out of the mother's hormone- treated body

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: It seems very simple but the woman has to take hormone injections daily and she also visits frequently the family planning clinic for several weeks

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: This period is very trying to her because the injections influence her very much, both physically and mentally

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: It is also trying on their relationship since the man has no opportunity to understand why his woman's temper change from one minute to another

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: The worst of it is the woman cannot understand either why her body has become unfamiliar to her

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: Thus it cannot be good for the body to be experimented with hormones like that

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: However, the experimentation is not ended with the hormone treatment

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: When they transplant the fertilized eggs into the womb they usually use two eggs and "if" the fertilization is a success it is not unusual with births of twins

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: Earlier when the clinics used as many as five eggs it was not uncommon with deliveries of quins

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: This is a health hazard considering how trying it is for the mother and her babies

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: My third argument is about peoples' aspiration of giving birth to a child of their own

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: The parents in the article are good examples of this aspiration since they went trough the strenuous test tube fertilisation and moreover they did the DNA test for they really must have a healthy child of their own genes

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I oppose this desire because that it is not given to everyone to succeed after only one treatment instead couples have to go through several years of afflictions and tears

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: During these years they are completely absorbed with the idea of having a child so they do not take care of other things that brighten the everyday life

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: Unfortunately, many couples have not managed to endure the pressure and their relations have broken up

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: I assert this striving is a waste of time and I do not think it is worth the suffering

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: To summarize, I will say that we cannot accept technology without restrictions

title: We have no right to experiment with human life
text: If we must use DNA technology as a foetus diagnostic it must be very limited

Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Henry Kissinger, former Secretary of State in the United States of America, argues in an article in Dagens Nyheter that massive military force must be used against the terrorist group Al-quaida, led by Usama bin Laden, which is believed to have been responsible of the attacks in New York and Washington on September the 11th this year

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: He gives an ultimatum; Afghanistan must hand over Usama bin Ladin and his adherents, or USA will feel free to attack the terrorist group as well as all those countries that are believed to give it shelter and economical aid

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: In this essay I will argue against Henry Kissinger, not against his claim to catch and punish those responsible for the terrorist attacks, but the very means that he wants his country and other western allies to use, namely massive military attacks

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Before I start arguing against this article I think it is appropriate to mention some points that I actually agree on

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Henry Kissinger suggests that a list of existing terror groups including the names of their members should be widely published and countries all over the world warned; that if they support these terrorists in any way at all they are going to face economical sanctions

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Neither should these persons be granted travel permits

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Finland has already taken such action: last year one of the terrorists who crashed in the Twin Towers in New York City actually applied for a visa to Finland

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: He was not allowed to enter the country because he appeared on the FBI's list of suspected terrorists

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: These kinds of actions, however, are not enough for Henry Kissinger

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Unlike him, I am not a supporter of the use of massive military force against targets in Afghanistan

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Firstly, it will be the death of thousands of Afghan civilians

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Bombs cannot tell the difference between a member of Al-quaida, Usama bin Ladin's terrorist group, and children

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Many of the bombs are so called "dummies", which are bombs that cannot be exactly targeted

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: As I write this many civilians have already died and US bombs have hit even United Nations and Red Cross buildings

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Secondly, acts of vengeance will only lead to new threats and attacks by the terrorists and result in a never-ending cycle of violence

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Today, the whole world is afraid of Anthrax spores distributed by mail and even more afraid of them being eventually distributed by air

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: The US air strikes provide these terrorists with new fuel to their hatred against, as they see it, the oppressive western world

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: What is more, it gives them an undeserved possibility to justify their evil deeds in front of the Afghans as well as the entire Islamic world

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: By using military retaliation the United States descends to the same criminal level as the terrorists

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Thirdly, even if Kissinger states that America should be cautious and not present this new policy as a battle between the West and Islam, it could still lead to an increased antagonism against the western world among Afghans and all surrounding countries

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Professor Ove Bring says that the right of self-defence must be cautiously used regarding the state in world politics in general

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: If not, the consequence will be anxiety and uncertainty around the world

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Again, with the use of hindsight, this is already to be seen, perhaps most clearly in Pakistan

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: American flags are there burnt in fierce demonstrations every day

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: The conflict between Palestine and Israel has escalated, hiding behind the attacks on Afghanistan

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: It is going to be very difficult to avoid that the Islamic part of the world sees these bombings as indirect attacks on it and the way of life it represents

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: My next argument against Henry Kissinger's article considers his view on moral justice and self-defence

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: It is only natural that USA has the right to defend itself when attacked

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: I am aware of the fact that the United Nation's article number 51 sanctions it with the right to attack non-civilian targets in a country that is supporting terrorism

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Still, if we assume that these bombings will be sustained over a long period in time, Afghan civilian casualties will be much larger in number than the corresponding number in Pentagon and the World Trade Centre, mainly due to the fact that it will be very difficult for humanitarian help to reach victims in need of food and medicine

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: If it comes to this, the principle on proportion will be out ruled

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: My conclusion must then be, even if I sincerely hope that I am wrong, that Henry Kissinger estimates American lives to be worth more than Afghan lives

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: In summary, I take it for granted that most of us know that terrorism develops from utter despair; it is the last weapon for people not seeing any other way out of their suffering

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Eradication trough bombings is not the solution, however

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: The only humane and reasonable way to end terrorism is to work for a world that provides welfare for all humans on equal terms

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Henry Kissinger, former Secretary of State in the United States of America, wants his country and its allies to use massive military force in the retaliation against the terrorist attacks on New York and Washington

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: USA should demand Afghanistan to hand over Usama Bin Ladin and his terror group Al-quaida

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Moreover, he wants USA to be very specific about the fact that it will not hesitate to attack any other country that harbours and protects terrorists within their territory

title: Terrorism cannot be bombed away
text: Kissinger emits a request to all supporters of this universal call to end terrorism, in order to end the bloodshed we must force the terrorist groups to flee and then destroy them

Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: In this argumentation essay I will present some arguments to why we should not privatise the study grant system

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I will also try to counter argue some of the arguments presented by the Muf

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Hopefully I will make you realize that we cannot let banks and private companies take over the system of study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Muf argues that the Csn should be abolished, the alternative would instead be to let banks and other private companies control the study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: They also suggest that if banks were in charge of loaning the money, the state would have to guarantee the repayment of the loans

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: This last suggestion is totally preposterous since it would mean that banks and other private companies would make all the profit and the state would have to stand for the loss of unsettled study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: If that isn't a welfare state what is? But only for the rich that is

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Moreover, I don't think it would be a good idea to let banks control the system of study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: A frightening scenario would be that banks grant loans only to people whom they see fit

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: It is of course more attractive to grant a loan to a student who wants to become a doctor than to one who wants to study free art

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The bank is more likely to get money back from the doctor than it is from the free art student

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I don't suggest the system we have to day is one of perfection

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: But I don't think we should abolish a system that after all has a good purpose and goal

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I agree that Csn has deficiencies, but I still say we give them one more chance in form of greatly increased financial resources and hopefully we will see a more generous study loan system in the future

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Muf also asserts in their article that the driving force for banks would be to gain profitable clients in the future

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: So be it, but we mustn't forget that, like all private agencies, banks want to make money as well

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: It would be naive to think that banks operate some kind of charity activity

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: For instance, will the bank extend their reception hours? 10:00 to 12:00 is not exactly the ideal time for a student to run bank errands

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Many people complain about getting unbelievably high charge bills sent home for not having paid in time

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: But how much will the bank charge you for the same bills? And how much will each reception visit cost you? One can of course always speculate what will and will not happened when a new system is being introduced

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: But like I mentioned, banks doesn't serve as benefactors, they are as greedy as anybody else on the free market

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Another argument presented by Muf to privatise the study loan system, is to raise the amount of money one is allowed to earn before having the grant reduced

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Muf also asserts in their article that if the amount of money earned exceeds the specific sum, no grant will be given at all

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: "With a normal income summer job one will soon exceed that sum, and that will result in getting no grant at all"

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The latter assertion is not even true

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The student is indeed given a grant, however, somewhat reduced

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I utterly agree in this matter, since I use to have problems with exceeding that amount myself

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Csn, or rather the government, has cured this ailment by raising the money you are allowed to earn per year from 54000 to 92500 Skr1

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I think that many people misguide their critique towards Csn

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The one to blame is actually the Swedish government because they are the ones who create all the laws and regulations

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: I have come to the conclusion that we cannot let banks and private companies take over the system of study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: A lot of smaller issues would probably not be solved just because banks take over the system, for instance, charge bills

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Anyhow, the most important issue is the one about elitism, the fact that banks can decide to which students they want to loan their money

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The result could be that only educations leading to well-paid jobs would be prioritised

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Summary: Muf argues that the Csn should be abolished, the alternative would instead be to let banks and other private companies control the study grants

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: The driving force for banks would be to gain profitable clients in the future

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Their main argument is that students are left out to a system that centralizes politics and not the students will and motive to study

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Another argument presented by Muf is that students cannot improve their financial situation by working extra (if exceeded, the grant will be reduced)

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Muf also asserts that it is arbitrary what education you get, and don't get study grants for

title: Should we privatise the system of study grants?
text: Lastly, Muf argues that by dividing the administration between several different actors, the students' power over their study situation will improve
